zh: 没有什么可以替代努力工作。
en: There is no substitute for hard work.

zh: 找到自我的最好方法就是在为他人服务中忘掉自我。
en: The best way to find yourself is to lose yourself in the service of others.

zh: 静以修身，俭以养德。
en: Quietude promotes learning, frugality cultivates virtue.

zh: 非淡泊无以明志，非宁静无以致远。
en: One can't show high ideals without a simple living; one can't have lofty aspirations without a peaceful state of mind.

zh: 要奋斗，要探索，要发现，而不要屈服。
en: To strive, to seek, to find and not to yield.

zh: 九月，是每个学生的新开始。
en: It's September, a new start for every student.

zh: 《青少年》杂志邀请了两位学生来写关于他们在高中的日常生活。
en: Teens has invited two students to write about their daily life in senior secondary school.

zh: 网络世界是我生活的一大部分。
en: The online world is a big part of my life.

zh: 我是一个“数字原住民”——我从小就开始使用电脑。
en: I'm a “digital native” — I've been using the computer since I was a kid.

zh: 我在网上做各种各样的事情。
en: I do a wide range of things online.

zh: 除了完成所有的学校作业，我还和朋友们聊天、看电影、阅读每日新闻和其他有趣的文章；我还在网上购买各种东西，比如书籍、电脑硬件和其他必需品。
en: Besides doing all my schoolwork, I chat with my friends, watch films and read the daily news and other interesting articles; I also shop for various things online, such as books, computer hardware and other necessities.

zh: 在购买之前，能够比较不同网店的质量和价格非常方便。
en: It’s so convenient to be able to compare the quality and prices from different online shops before I buy.

zh: 我偶尔也玩电脑游戏，我甚至还在客厅的电视屏幕上打网球！
en: I also play computer games from time to time and I even play tennis on my television screen in my living room!

zh: 我的父母担心我可能会变成一个“网瘾者”。
en: My parents are worried that I may become an “Internet addict”.

zh: 他们认为我一直在玩电脑游戏，并且和网友聊天太多。
en: They think I'm playing computer games all the time and I chat too much with online friends.

zh: 据他们说，存在一种危险，即我可能无法分辨这些朋友是否是真正的朋友。
en: According to them, there is a danger that I may not be able to tell whether these friends are real friends.

zh: 我妈妈一直告诉我要和学校的朋友们出去玩。
en: My mum keeps telling me to go out with my school friends instead.

zh: 时不时地亲自去见朋友是很重要的，而不仅仅是在社交媒体上。
en: It's important to meet friends in person from time to time, not just on social media.

zh: 实际上，我确实知道我需要有时强迫自己离开网络世界，特别是因为现实生活也同样有趣。
en: Actually, I do know I need to drag myself away from the online world sometimes, especially because real life can be just as interesting.

zh: 高中是一个新的开始。
en: Senior secondary school is a new start.

zh: 学习无疑是我生活中的一大部分。
en: Studying is definitely a big part of my life.

zh: 我倾向于在每个学期初为每个科目设定一个目标。
en: I tend to set a goal for every subject at the beginning of each term.

zh: 我的目标是这学期每门科目都取得好成绩。
en: My aim is to do well in every subject this term.

zh: 生物是我最喜欢的科目，我的目标是为大学攻读生物学位做好准备。
en: Biology is my favourite subject, and my target is to prepare myself for my degree in biology at university.

zh: 我在所有课堂上都很专心并积极思考，这样放学后我就能有更多空闲时间做我感兴趣的其他事情。
en: I am always attentive in all classes and think actively, so that I can have more free time to do other things that I'm interested in after school.

zh: 我喜欢阅读各种书籍。
en: I like reading books of all kinds.

zh: 我有一个前十的阅读清单，并努力保持更新。
en: I have a top 10 reading list and I try to keep it updated.

zh: 同时，我是学校长跑队和志愿者俱乐部的活跃成员。
en: Meanwhile, I'm an active member of my school's long-distance running team and volunteering club.

zh: 努力工作并共同实现团队目标总是让我感到兴奋。
en: It always makes me excited to work hard and achieve a team goal together.

zh: 为了实现我想要的一切，我必须好好利用我的时间。
en: To achieve all I want, I must use my time well.

zh: 在学校，我有空余时间就做作业。
en: At school, I do my homework when I have spare time.

zh: 放学后，我尝试利用任何可能的时间复习白天学到的东西。
en: After school, I try to use any possible time to revise the things I have learnt during the day.

zh: 例如，我通常坐公交车回家需要20分钟。
en: For example, it usually takes me 20 minutes to get home by bus.

zh: 我经常利用这个时间复习英语单词。
en: I often use the time to review English words.

zh: 正如俗话所说，“分秒必争！”
en: As the popular saying goes, “Every minute counts!”

zh: 设定目标，取得进步！
en: Set a goal and get ahead!

zh: 乔是一个“数字原住民”。
en: Joe is a “digital native”.

zh: 他在网上做很多事情，例如与朋友聊天。
en: He does lots of things online, for example chatting with his friends.

zh: 乔的父母担心他可能会成为“网瘾者”，因为他们认为他一直在玩电脑游戏，并且与网友聊天太多。
en: Joe's parents are worried that he may become an “Internet addict”, because they think he plays computer games all the time and chats too much with online friends.

zh: 他们还担心他可能无法分辨这些朋友是否是真正的朋友。
en: They are also worried that he may not be able to tell whether these friends are real friends.

zh: 事实上，乔知道他应该花更多时间享受现实生活。
en: In fact Joe knows he should spend more time enjoying real life.

zh: 我妈妈一直告诉我要和我的学校朋友一起出去玩。
en: My mum keeps telling me to go out with my school friends instead.

zh: 时不时地亲自与朋友见面是很重要的，而不仅仅是在社交媒体上。
en: It's important to meet friends in person from time to time, not just on social media.

zh: 当我着手做某事时，我会尽我最大的努力去实现它。
en: When I set out to do something, I do my best to achieve it.

zh: 我的目标是为我在大学的生物学学位做准备。
en: My target is to prepare myself for my degree in biology at university.

zh: 李颖是她们班第一个加入志愿者俱乐部的人。
en: Li Ying is the first person to join the volunteering club in her class.

zh: 我每天早上六点醒来。
en: I wake up at six o'clock every morning.

zh: 我总是第一个到教室的人。
en: I’m always the first person to get to my class.

zh: 早上总是很忙，下午甚至更忙！
en: The mornings are always very busy and the afternoons are even busier!

zh: 大约五点钟，我通常会找时间做文书工作和预习功课。
en: By around five o'clock, I usually find time to do my paperwork and preview the lessons.

zh: 你可能会认为每天做同样的事情很无聊，但我喜欢忙碌。
en: You may think that it is boring to do the same thing every day, but I like being busy.

zh: 张天带着许多书籍、衣服和两双运动鞋，怀着一颗热切的心来到了村庄。
en: Bringing with him lots of books, clothes, and two pairs of trainers, Zhang Tian travelled to the village with an eager heart.

zh: 他想象了关于独立生活和在村庄教书的各种激动人心的事情。
en: He imagined all sorts of exciting things about living independently and teaching in a village.

zh: 然而，并非一切都符合张天的期望。
en: However, not everything lived up to Zhang Tian’s hopes.

zh: 学校比他预期的要小得多，只有三间教室。
en: The school was much smaller than he had expected, with only three classrooms.

zh: 教室前面有一个操场，刮风天会尘土飞扬，下雨天则会泥泞不堪。
en: In front of the classrooms, there was a playground which got dusty on windy days and muddy on rainy days.

zh: 住在村里也比他想像的更具挑战性。
en: Living in the village was also more challenging than he had thought.

zh: 水电供应不稳定，所以他只能每三四天洗一次澡，并且不得不学习如何做饭。
en: The power and water supplies were unstable, so he could only shower every three or four days, and he had to learn how to cook.

zh: 离开的念头曾在他脑海中闪过，但他很快放弃了这个想法，并找到了应对挑战的方法。
en: The thought of leaving once flashed through his mind, but he quickly gave up on the idea and found ways to deal with the challenges.

zh: 学校只有三名教师，张天是唯一的英语老师。
en: The school had just three teachers and Zhang Tian was the only English teacher.

zh: 另外两名本地老师负责数学和语文。
en: The other two local teachers were responsible for maths and Chinese.

zh: 为了让学生的校园生活更健康、更有活力，张天为学校引入了更多的科目——音乐、美术和体育。
en: To make school life healthier and livelier for his students, Zhang Tian introduced more subjects to the school — music, art and PE.

zh: 体育是孩子们最喜欢的科目，这并不奇怪！
en: It is not surprising that PE is the kids' favourite subject!

zh: 他们现在的校园生活更有吸引力也更有趣，他们喜欢在操场上踢足球，也喜欢唱他们学过的歌曲。
en: Their school lives are now more attractive and interesting, and they enjoy playing football in the playground, as well as singing songs they've learnt.

zh: 学校里现在充满了欢声笑语和音乐。
en: The school is now full of laughter and music.

zh: 张天正计划组织有史以来的第一场校园音乐会！
en: Zhang Tian is planning to organise the first-ever school concert!

zh: 张天的第一年快要结束了。
en: Zhang Tian’s first year is almost over.

zh: 这是艰难的一年，但他很享受和孩子们一起工作。
en: It has been a tough year, but he has enjoyed working with the children.

zh: 让他感到满意的是，他的学生们能够用英语读、说和写，并且他们在学习上变得更加自信。
en: What made him feel satisfied was that his students were able to read, speak and write in English, and they became more confident in learning.

zh: 除了教学，张天也给村庄带来了变化。
en: Besides teaching, Zhang Tian also brought changes to the village.

zh: 他联系了慈善组织，商讨重建操场和建立图书馆的事宜，并帮助村民在线销售当地产品。
en: He contacted charity organisations about rebuilding the playground and setting up a library, and helped the villagers sell local products online.

zh: 他为村庄做出的贡献是巨大的，因此他在村民中非常受欢迎，他们把他当作自己人。
en: The contribution he made to the village was great, so he became very popular among the villagers and they treated him as one of them.

zh: 现在，这个村庄就像他的第二个家。
en: The village is like his second home now.

zh: 虽然他原本只打算待一年，但现在他觉得可以再待一年。
en: Although he had only intended to stay for one year, he now feels ready to stay for another year.

zh: 他感到非常高兴，因为他在选择人生道路时追随了自己的内心，尽管这可能不是别人所期望的。
en: He feels so happy that he followed his heart when choosing what to do with his life, even though it may not be what others expected of him.

zh: 你在加拿大的生活怎么样？我希望你已经很好地适应了新环境，并且喜欢你的新学校。
en: How's your life in Canada? I hope you have adapted well to the new situation and are enjoying your new school.

zh: 我爱我的新高中。
en: I love my new senior secondary school.

zh: 它比我的初中大得多，还有一个巨大的体育馆！
en: It's much bigger than my junior secondary school and there's a huge gym!

zh: 你还记得谢子君吗？
en: Do you remember Xie Zijun?

zh: 你猜怎么着——我们在同一所学校！
en: Guess what — we are in the same school!

zh: 有个朋友总是好的，对吧？
en: It’s always good to have a friend, right?

zh: 高中的生活和初中的生活非常不同！
en: Life in senior secondary school is very different from that in junior secondary school!

zh: 你知道，我们没有固定的教室！
en: You know, we don't have a fixed classroom!

zh: 我们去不同的班级上不同的科目，也上不同水平的课程。
en: We go to different classes for different subjects and also for different levels.

zh: 我和子君在英语和物理课上在同一个班，但在语文和数学课上在不同的班。
en: I'm in the same class with Zijun for English and physics but in different classes for Chinese and maths.

zh: 课后我们还有很多不同的社团。
en: We also have many different clubs after class.

zh: 你知道，我喜欢跳舞，所以我参加了舞蹈社。
en: You know, I like dancing, so I'm in the Dancing Club.

zh: 目前，我们每周见面两次，我们正在准备参加一个比赛。
en: At the moment, we meet twice a week and we're preparing to attend a competition.

zh: 我认为我的新学校给了我一个成为不同的人的机会，我喜欢这个“新的我”。
en: I think my new school gives me a chance to become a different person and I like the “new me”.

zh: 好了，我现在必须走了。
en: Well, I must go now.

zh: 我明天有一个演讲，我需要完成我的幻灯片。
en: I've got a presentation tomorrow and I need to finish my slides.

zh: 我期待着尽快听到你的一切近况。
en: I look forward to hearing all about what's happening with you soon.

zh: 这是一个关于美国航空先驱阿梅莉亚·埃尔哈特的故事，她是历史上第一位独自飞越大西洋的女飞行员。
en: This is a story about American aviation pioneer Amelia Earhart, the first female pilot to fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean in history.

zh: “当我离地两三百英尺时，我知道我必须飞行。”
en: “By the time I had got two or three hundred feet off the ground, I knew I had to fly.”

zh: 茶歇不仅仅是一杯温暖的好饮料。
en: There's more to teatime than just a nice warm drink.

zh: 不同的文化将茶与不同的食物或小吃相结合，使茶歇成为一种社交场合。
en: Different cultures combine tea with different foods or snacks, turning teatime into a social occasion.

zh: 人们认为一位英国公爵夫人首先引入了下午茶的概念。
en: People believe that an English Duchess first introduced the idea of afternoon tea.

zh: 她的名字是安娜·罗素（1783-1857），她是贝德福德第七公爵夫人。
en: Her name was Anna Russell (1783-1857), and she was the 7th Duchess of Bedford.

zh: 在18世纪和19世纪，大多数英国人每天只吃两顿主餐——早上的早餐和一顿丰盛的晚餐，这顿晚餐通常要到晚上七点才供应。
en: In the 18th and 19th centuries, most English people ate only two main meals each day — breakfast in the morning and a large supper which wasn't usually served until seven o'clock in the evening.

zh: 因此，人们在这两餐之间的长时间休息中经常感到非常饥饿。
en: As a result, people often got very hungry during the long break between these two meals.

zh: 公爵夫人想出了一个聪明的办法来解决这个问题。
en: The Duchess came up with a clever idea to solve this problem.

zh: 她邀请了一些朋友和她一起共进下午餐。
en: She invited some of her friends to join her for an afternoon meal.

zh: 这顿饭在四点到五点之间举行。
en: The meal was held between four and five o'clock.

zh: 它包括蛋糕和三明治，并提供茶来帮助吞咽食物。
en: It included cakes and sandwiches, and tea was served to wash down the food.

zh: 为了使下午餐更加重要，她使用了细瓷制成的杯子和盘子，以及银制的茶壶、刀、叉和勺子。
en: To make the afternoon meal more important, she used cups and plates made from fine china, and teapots, knives, forks and spoons made from silver.

zh: 下午茶派对很快成为流行的社交场合，这一趋势一直持续到今天。
en: Afternoon tea parties soon became popular social occasions, which is a trend that continues today.

zh: 它们在现代英国的社交生活中仍然扮演着重要的角色。
en: They still play an important part in the social life in modern Britain.

zh: 去饮茶或吃点心是一种在西方世界越来越流行的做法——但它在中国南方有着悠久的历史。
en: Going for yum cha or dim sum is a practice that has become increasingly popular in the western world — but it has a long history in southern China.

zh: 饮茶字面意思是“喝茶”，它是在餐厅吃点心的做法。
en: Yum cha literally means "drink tea", and it is the practice of eating dim sum at a restaurant.

zh: 点心由小份的食物组成，可以是蒸的或炸的。
en: Dim sum consists of small-sized portions of food, either steamed or fried.

zh: 人们与家人和朋友一起去饮茶，在轻松的氛围中社交和聊天。
en: People go for yum cha with family and friends to socialise and catch up in a laidback atmosphere.

zh: 在西方，最受欢迎的饮茶时间大约是所谓的“早午餐”时间。
en: The most popular time to go for yum cha in the West is around what is known as "brunch".

zh: 事实上，有人说早午餐的灵感来自饮茶。
en: In fact, some say that brunch was inspired by yum cha.

zh: 据信，这个习俗始于数千年前，当时茶馆开始沿着丝绸之路涌现，为疲倦的旅行者提供休息。
en: It is believed that the custom began thousands of years ago when teahouses started cropping up along the Silk Road to provide rest for weary travellers.

zh: 人们发现提供食物可以增加生意，所以茶馆老板开始提供小吃配茶，饮茶就这样诞生了。
en: It was discovered that offering food increased business, so teahouse owners began serving snacks with their tea and yum cha was born.

zh: 因为我爸爸的工作，我一直在从一个城市搬到另一个城市。
en: I've been moving from one city to the next because of my dad's job.

zh: 我们从香港开始，然后搬到北京，接着是新加坡，然后是旧金山。
en: We started in Hong Kong, then moved to Beijing, then Singapore, and then San Francisco.

zh: 现在我们住在伦敦。
en: Now we live in London.

zh: 我喜欢城市生活，因为它充满了文化、娱乐、教育和娱乐的机会。
en: I love city life because it's full of opportunities for culture, recreation, education and entertainment.

zh: 在伦敦，我的家离博物馆、动物园、电影院和社区中心很近，所以我在空闲时间有很多事情可做。
en: In London, my home is very close to museums, the zoo, cinemas and community centres, so I have lots to do in my free time.

zh: 此外，因为我住在公共图书馆和书店附近，我总是有一本好书可以读。
en: Also, as I live close to public libraries and bookshops, I always have a good book to read.

zh: 如果我感到无聊，我可以轻松找到稳定的互联网连接。
en: And if I'm ever bored, I can easily find a stable Internet connection.

zh: 住在乡村的人说城市生活因为所有的噪音和交通而压力很大，但我喜欢置身于事物之中。
en: People who live in the country say that city life is stressful because of all the noise and traffic, but I like being in the middle of things.

zh: 此外，你总能找到一些宁静和平和，因为大多数城市都有可爱、宁静的公园。
en: Besides, you can always find some peace and calm because most cities have lovely, peaceful parks.

zh: 城市人认为乡村生活一定很无聊，但这不是真的。
en: City folk think life in the country must be boring but this isn’t true.

zh: 当我的家人在怀特岛（英格兰海岸附近的一个岛屿）度过一周时，我学到了这一点。
en: I learnt this when my family spent a week on the Isle of Wight (an island off the coast of England).

zh: 我们进行了自然漫步，在美丽的海滩上野餐，并在星空下喝热巧克力。
en: We went on nature walks, had picnics on beautiful beaches and drank hot chocolate under the stars.

zh: 到周末时，我喜欢上了住在乡村地区。
en: By the end of the week, I loved living in a rural area.

zh: 当你周围到处都是动物时，你不需要去动物园。
en: You don’t need to visit zoos when there are animals all around you.

zh: 我喂了牛、山羊和鸡，甚至学会了骑马。
en: I fed cows, goats and chickens, and even learnt to ride a horse.

zh: 你不需要参观博物馆、电影院或社区中心，因为你可以探索森林、洞穴和海滩。
en: You don't need to visit museums, cinemas or community centres because you can explore forests, caves and beaches.

zh: 我没有错过上网，因为我太忙于与大自然联系了！
en: I didn’t miss going online because I was too busy connecting with nature!

zh: 城市生活可能很孤独
en: City Life Can Be Lonely

zh: 一些城市人认为乡村生活是孤独的，因为你与外界隔绝。
en: Some city people think country life is lonely because you're cut off from the outside world.

zh: 他们不知道远离拥挤的街道和繁忙的购物中心是多么的宁静。
en: They don’t know how peaceful it is to be away from crowded streets and busy shopping centres.

zh: 他们也不知道乡村城镇和村庄的人们可以多么友好。
en: They also don’t know how friendly people in country towns and villages can be.

zh: 如果有区别的话，人们更可能在一个大城市里感到孤独。
en: If anything, people are more likely to be lonely in a big city.

zh: 我在乡村一周交到的朋友比我在伦敦头三个月交到的还要多！
en: I made more friends in a week in the country than I made during my first three months in London!

zh: 那么哪个更好——城市还是乡村？
en: So which is better — the city or the country?

zh: 我认为这不在于你住在哪里——而在于你如何生活。
en: I think it isn’t about where you live — it's about how you live.

zh: 我目前住在一个城市，但由于我们的岛屿假期，我相信我在乡村生活也能同样快乐。
en: I live in a city for now, but thanks to our island holiday, I'm sure I could be just as happy living in the country.

zh: 我是一名医生。我的工作是照顾人们的健康。
en: I'm a doctor. My job is to take care of people's health.

zh: 当我看到我的病人康复时，我感到很满足。
en: I feel satisfied when I see my patients recover.

zh: 但我的生活一直充满压力。
en: But my life is stressful all the time.

zh: 我必须很早就起床，并且经常很晚才回家，感觉很累。
en: I have to get up very early in the morning and often go back home late, feeling tired.

zh: 我只有在周末才有时间和我的孩子们玩耍。
en: I only have time to play with my children on weekends.

zh: 由于压力，我晚上睡不好觉。
en: Due to the stress, I cannot sleep well at night.

zh: 成为一名志愿教师曾是我的梦想。
en: It was my dream to become a volunteer teacher.

zh: 所以三年前，我离开了家乡，在湖南省的一个村庄教英语。
en: So three years ago, I left my hometown and taught English in a village in Hunan Province.

zh: 和孩子们一起工作很令人兴奋。
en: It is exciting to work with kids.

zh: 但在村里当老师可能具有挑战性，这并不令人惊讶。
en: But it is not surprising that being a teacher in the village can be challenging.

zh: 电力和供水不稳定。
en: The power and water supplies are unstable.

zh: 此外，外出很不方便，因为公共交通连接不畅。
en: Besides, it is inconvenient to go outside because the public transportation is not well connected.

zh: 我已经一年多没见到我的父母了。
en: I haven't seen my parents for more than one year.

zh: 我非常想念他们。
en: I miss them a lot.

zh: 我是一名初中生。
en: I'm a junior secondary school student.

zh: 我刚来这所新学校几周。
en: I've just come to a new school for a few weeks.

zh: 这些天我感到很沮丧。
en: I feel depressed these days.

zh: 我很害羞，所以社交场合对我来说很有压力。
en: I'm very shy, so social situations are stressful for me.

zh: 我妈妈一直告诉我要和我的同学交新朋友。
en: My mother keeps telling me to make new friends with my classmates.

zh: 我知道那一定很有趣。
en: I know it must be interesting.

zh: 但对我来说，和新同学聊天总是那么困难。
en: But it is always so hard for me to chat with new classmates.

zh: 我开始担心我在这里的生活。
en: I start to worry about my life here.

zh: 我非常想念我的老朋友。
en: I miss my old friends so much.

zh: 真正的享受来自于心灵的活跃和身体的锻炼；二者永远结合在一起。
en: True enjoyment comes from activity of the mind and exercise of the body; the two are ever united.

zh: 健康和幸福不仅依赖于饮食，还依赖于运动和锻炼。
en: Health and wellness are dependent not only on diet, but also on sport and exercise.

zh: 你必须学习游戏的规则。
en: You have to learn the rules of the game.

zh: 然后你必须比任何人都打得更好。
en: And then you have to play better than anyone else.

zh: 嗯，粉丝们总是支持弱者。
en: Well, fans always root for the underdog.

zh: 保罗和我在我们的学校篮球队，狮子队。
en: Paul and I were on our school basketball team, The Lions.

zh: 我们热爱篮球，并且都是NBA的超级粉丝。
en: We loved basketball and were both huge fans of the NBA.

zh: 当我们不在我们大楼旁边的球场上打球时，我们就在电视上看比赛。
en: When we weren't playing on the court which was next to our building, we were watching a game on TV.

zh: 我最喜欢的球员是勒布朗·詹姆斯。
en: My favourite player was LeBron James.

zh: 保罗最喜欢的球员是泰龙·博格斯，一个为夏洛特黄蜂队效力的家伙，尽管我们实际上都同意他们都是冠军。
en: Paul's favourite player was Tyrone Bogues, a guy who played for the Charlotte Hornets, although we actually agree that they are both champions.

zh: 博格斯只有1.6米高，这使他成为NBA有史以来最矮的球员。
en: Bogues was only 1.6 metres tall, which made him the shortest player ever in the NBA.

zh: 猜猜怎么着？保罗也只有1.6米高！
en: Guess what? Paul was only 1.6 metres tall, too!

zh: 保罗知道比其他球员矮意味着他必须更加努力地练习。
en: Paul knew that being shorter than other players meant that he had to practise more.

zh: 在他独自练习跳投的所有时间里，他以博格斯为他的灵感。
en: During all those hours of doing jump shots on his own, he used Bogues as his inspiration.

zh: 保罗曾经说过，“如果博格斯能做到，为什么我不行？”
en: Paul once said, “If Bogues could make it, why not me?”

zh: 我们的教练并不那么确定。
en: Our coach was not so sure.

zh: 保罗为了进入球队不得不尝试了很多次。
en: Paul had to try out many times just for making the team.

zh: 他通常仍然坐在替补席上，只是一个替补，这对他来说真的很艰难。
en: He was still usually on the bench, being just a replacement, which was really tough on him.

zh: 每个人都知道保罗有真正的技术，并且是一个工作非常努力、有强烈愿望为球队效力的人。
en: Everyone knew Paul had real skills, and was someone who worked really hard and had a strong desire to play for the team.

zh: 然而，保罗没有得到机会。
en: However, Paul didn't get a chance.

zh: 这周，狮子队正在与我们主要的竞争对手，熊队比赛，这是一支本赛季记录完美的球队。
en: This week, The Lions were playing our main competitors, The Bears, a team whose record this season had been perfect.

zh: 他们一场比赛都没有输过。
en: They hadn't lost a single game.

zh: 这将是一场艰难的比赛。
en: It would be a tough one.

zh: 保罗不知道他很快就会得到他一直在等待的机会。
en: Paul didn't know he'd soon get the chance that he'd been waiting for.

zh: 突然，一名球员和我撞在了一起。
en: Suddenly, a player and I crashed into each other.

zh: 疼痛瞬间传遍了我的全身。
en: Pain raced through my body.

zh: 队员们围拢过来，看起来很担心。
en: The team gathered around, looking worried.

zh: 最后一节比赛即将开始，而我的队伍落后10分。
en: The last quarter was about to begin, and my team was behind by 10 points.

zh: “教练，我想我不能再打了，”当医生在我膝盖上放冰袋时，我轻声说道。
en: “I don't think I can play anymore, coach,” I said quietly as the doctor put an ice pack on my knee.

zh: “不行，”教练严厉地回答道。“我们没有其他球员了！”
en: “No way,” the coach replied sharply. “We've got no more players!”

zh: “保罗怎么样？”有人建议道。
en: “What about Paul?” someone suggested.

zh: “保罗？”教练说。“他不会打球！”
en: “Paul?” said the coach. “He can't play!”

zh: “给他个机会吧，教练！”我说。“我们还有什么可输的呢？”
en: “Give him a shot, coach!” I said. “What have we got to lose?”

zh: “说得对，”另一名球员说。“反正我们也要输了。让保罗上吧！”
en: “That's right,” another player said. “We're losing anyway. Let Paul play!”

zh: “让我试试吧，教练！我不会让你失望的！”保罗说。
en: “Let me try, coach! I won't let you down!” Paul said.

zh: “好吧，好吧！”教练终于同意了，“别让我们失望，保罗。这是你闪耀的时刻。”
en: “OK, OK!” the coach finally agreed, “Don't let us down, Paul. It’s your time to shine.”

zh: 保罗跳起来冲进了球场。
en: Paul jumped up and rushed onto the court.

zh: 显然，他独自练习所付出的所有额外时间都得到了回报。
en: And clearly, all the extra hours that he'd spent practising alone paid off.

zh: 另一支队伍根本跟不上他的能量和速度。
en: The other team just couldn't keep up with his energy and speed.

zh: 他投进了一个又一个球，观众们不停地鼓掌和欢呼。
en: He made shot after shot, and the crowd couldn't stop clapping and cheering.

zh: 当比赛结束时，我们队以两分的优势获胜。
en: When the game ended, our team had won by two points.

zh: “嗯，”教练拍着保罗的肩膀说，“你刚刚为自己在队里赢得了一席之地，大个子！”
en: “Well,” said the coach as he hit Paul on the shoulder, “you've just earned your place on the team, big guy!”

zh: 一名球员受伤了，无法比赛。
en: One player got hurt and could not play.

zh: 担心地喊道：“我们没有更多的球员了！”
en: Worried C'We’ve got no more players!)

zh: 威尔：你好，是约翰吗？我是威尔。
en: Will: Hello, is that John? It's Will.

zh: 约翰：嗨，威尔。怎么了？
en: John: Hi, Will. What's up?

zh: 威尔：我知道你最近有很多事要忙，所以你大概没时间看今晚曼联和利物浦之间的比赛。
en: Will: I know you've had lots to occupy your time, so you probably didn't have time to watch the match between Manchester United and Liverpool tonight.

zh: 约翰：啊！那场比赛！我错过了。谁赢了？
en: John: Ah! The match! I missed it. Who won?

zh: 威尔：利物浦！我们三比二赢了。我们的防守非常出色！
en: Will: Liverpool! We won three-two. Our defence was excellent!

zh: 约翰：利物浦！三比二？真的吗？我简直不敢相信！我们上次打得太差了！那真是太令人失望了。
en: John: Liverpool! Three-two? Really? I can't believe it! We played so poorly last time! It was such a disappointment.

zh: 威尔：是啊，我知道，但这是真的！球队表现得非常出色。我为自己是粉丝感到非常自豪！
en: Will: Yeah, I know, but it's true! The team played amazingly. I'm so proud to be a fan!

zh: 正如人们常说的，任何锻炼都比不锻炼好，但长跑尤其有很多好处。
en: As people often say, any exercise is better than none, but long-distance running in particular has a lot of benefits.

zh: 这是一项非常适合初学者的运动——你不需要健身房会员资格或任何特殊设备。
en: It is a great sport for beginners — you do not need a gym membership or any special equipment.

zh: 只需要一双好的跑鞋就可以了。
en: Just a pair of good running shoes will do.

zh: 也没有更好的方法去了解自己，看看自己有什么能力。
en: There is also no better way to know yourself and to see what you are capable of.

zh: 你也是一个担心生病的人。
en: You are also somebody who worries about getting sick.

zh: 跑步将帮助你保持健康并预防疾病：它会给你很好的上肢和下肢力量。
en: Running will help you get fit and prevent diseases: It will give you good upper and lower body strength.

zh: 和任何运动一样，跑步会增加流向大脑的血液和氧气。
en: Like any exercise, running increases the flow of blood and oxygen to your brain.

zh: 它使你的心脏更强壮，让更多的血液在全身流动。
en: It makes your heart stronger and allows more blood to flow around your body.

zh: 它还能增强你的免疫系统，降低患上严重健康问题以及像感冒这样更常见疾病的风险。
en: It also strengthens your immune system and reduces the risk of serious health problems, as well as more common illnesses like colds.

zh: 此外，任何情绪低落的人都可以去跑步来让自己振作起来。
en: Besides, anybody who is feeling down can go for a run to cheer themselves up.

zh: 即使是三十分钟的跑步也能缓解你可能因压力而遭受的疼痛或紧张。
en: Even a thirty-minute run will provide relief from aches or tension that you may be suffering due to stress.

zh: 如果你是跑步新手，这里有一些给你的建议——在跑步前后进行适当的热身和冷身很重要，这样你就不会伤害到自己。
en: If you are new to running, here is some advice for you — it is important to warm up and cool down properly before and after running, so you do not hurt yourself.

zh: 热身能让你的血液流动起来，并在你锻炼前让身体做好准备。
en: A warm-up gets your blood flowing and prepares your body before you exercise.

zh: 长跑之后，你应该慢跑十分钟，然后步行五分钟。
en: After a long run, you should jog slowly for ten minutes, then walk for five minutes.

zh: 这种冷身运动可以在锻炼后放松你的身体。
en: Such kind of warm-down relaxes your body after exercise.

zh: 更多关于跑步的指南，请点击这里。
en: For more guidelines on running, click here.

zh: 我希望这能有所帮助！
en: I hope this will help!

zh: 制定一个适合你的训练计划。
en: Draw up a training plan that is right for you.

zh: 没有捷径——没有人能一夜之间成为长跑运动员！
en: There are no shortcuts — no one can become a long-distance runner overnight!

zh: 例如，开始时只慢跑二十分钟。
en: For example, jogging for only twenty minutes to start with.

zh: 确保你的跑步量是你的心脏、肌肉和骨骼能够承受而不受伤的。
en: Make sure you are running the right amount that your heart, muscles and bones can take without getting hurt.

zh: 跑步既锻炼你的身体，也锻炼你的意志。
en: Running trains your mind as well as your body.

zh: 会有你想放弃的时候。
en: There will be times when you want to give up.

zh: 但坚持下去，告诉自己随着时间的推移会变得更容易。
en: But hang in there and tell yourself that it will get easier with time.

zh: 没有什么比意识到所有的汗水和努力最终都是值得的更让人满足的了。
en: Nothing gives one more satisfaction than realising that all the sweat and hard work were worth it in the end.

zh: 别忘了休息。
en: Do not forget to take breaks.

zh: 训练过多可能对健康有害，所以确保不要过度。
en: Training too much can be bad for your health, so make sure you do not overdo it.

zh: 我小时候总是生病，而且讨厌做运动。
en: I was always sick as a kid and I hated doing sports.

zh: 我宁愿做任何事也不愿锻炼。
en: I would rather do anything other than exercise.

zh: 如果你告诉我儿时的朋友我现在是一个跑步爱好者，没有人会相信你。
en: If you told my childhood friends that I am now a lover of running, nobody would believe you.

zh: 但当我进入大学时，我内心的一些东西开始改变了。
en: But something in me started to change when I got into university.

zh: 在第一周，我一个人都不认识，所以我总是独自一人。
en: During my first week, I didn't know anybody, so I was alone all the time.

zh: 就在那时，我意识到跑步是一个结交朋友和在你独自一人时消磨时间的好方法，并且在事情变得有压力时，没有什么比一次好的跑步更能让你清醒头脑了。
en: That's when I realised that running is a great way to make friends and kill time when you're alone, and there's nothing better than a good run to clear your head when things get stressful.

zh: 开始跑步和锻炼永远不会太晚——毕竟，俗话说：“一切皆有可能！”
en: It's never too late to start getting into running and exercising — after all, as the saying goes, “Anything is possible!”

zh: 这是西班牙圣巴巴拉XV大奖赛自行车越野赛的最后冲刺阶段。
en: It was the final sprint of the Santa Barbara XV Grand Prix cyclo-cross race in Spain.

zh: 粉丝们排列在通往终点线的道路两旁，为选手们加油助威。
en: Fans lined the road to the finishing line, cheering on the competitors.

zh: 处于第三名的伊斯梅尔·埃斯特班仅领先于他的竞争对手奥古斯丁·纳瓦罗，距离终点线只有300米。
en: Ismael Esteban, in third place, was just ahead of his competitor, Agustin Navarro, with only 300 metres to the finishing line.

zh: 埃斯特班已经尽全力骑行了数小时，随着终点线在望，所有自行车手都开始加速通过。
en: Esteban had been cycling as hard as he could for hours and with the finishing line in sight all the cyclists began to speed up to cross it.

zh: 然后，埃斯特班突然爆胎了。
en: Then, suddenly Esteban got a puncture.

zh: 他该怎么办？放弃还是继续？
en: What should he do? Give up or continue?

zh: 埃斯特班把自行车扛在肩上，向终点线冲去。
en: Esteban threw his bike over his shoulders and raced towards the finishing line.

zh: 几秒钟后，排名第四的纳瓦罗追上了他。
en: Just a few seconds later, fourth-placed Navarro caught up with him.

zh: 当然，他本可以轻松超过埃斯特班并获得第三名。
en: Surely, he could simply overtake Esteban and come third.

zh: 但纳瓦罗放慢了速度，让埃斯特班跑在他前面。
en: But Navarro slowed down and let Esteban run ahead of him.

zh: 两位自行车手到达了终点线，埃斯特班徒步领先，纳瓦罗紧随其后。
en: The two cyclists reached the finishing line with Esteban ahead on foot, Navarro just behind him.

zh: 纳瓦罗让埃斯特班以第三名完赛，而他自己则获得第四名。
en: Navarro had let Esteban finish in third place, while he came fourth.

zh: 观众对他展现的体育精神感到惊讶，并为自行车手们欢呼鼓掌。
en: The audience was amazed at his show of sportsmanship and cheered and clapped for the cyclists.

zh: 赛后，纳瓦罗说：“我不想以那种方式获胜。”
en: After the race, Navarro said, "I didn't want to win that way."

zh: 后来，赢得铜牌的埃斯特班试图将他的奖牌给纳瓦罗。
en: Later, Esteban, who won the bronze medal, tried to give his medal to Navarro.

zh: 然而，纳瓦罗拒绝了，他的回应是：“你把奖牌给我，这比我对你所做的更有价值。”
en: However, Navarro refused, and his response was: "Offering me the prize was worth more than what I did for him."

zh: 两位自行车手做出了惊人的体育姿态——纳瓦罗拒绝利用埃斯特班的困境，而埃斯特班则将自己的奖牌赠予纳瓦罗。
en: The two cyclists made amazing sporting gestures — Navarro for refusing to take advantage of Esteban's troubles, and Esteban for offering Navarro his medal.

zh: 这是体育精神的最佳体现。
en: This was sportsmanship at its very best.

zh: 童年是培养享受健康体育活动乐趣的绝佳时期。
en: Childhood is the perfect time to key in on the enjoyment of healthy physical activity.

zh: 但随着孩子年龄增长，父母，有时是教练，可能会将孩子对某项特定运动或舞蹈的参与推到身体受伤的程度。
en: But as children get older, parents, and sometimes coaches, may push a child's involvement in a particular sport or dance to the point of physical injury.

zh: 当今最流行的武术起源于亚洲，尤其是中国。
en: The most popular martial arts today originated in Asia, especially in China.

zh: 一些中国格斗技法的发明可以追溯到3000多年前。
en: Some Chinese fighting styles were invented more than 3,000 years ago.

zh: 许多武术被发明出来是因为人们需要自卫或保护他人。
en: Many martial arts were invented because people needed to defend themselves or protect others.

zh: 如今，人们学习武术是为了健身或作为一种竞技运动，但它们对于自卫仍然非常有用。
en: Nowadays, people learn martial arts as a way of keeping fit or as a competitive sport, but they are still very useful for self-defence.

zh: 武术通常被分为软和硬两种。
en: Martial arts are often referred to as either soft or hard.

zh: 这些术语暗示了一种应对对手力量的方式。
en: These terms imply a way of dealing with your opponent's force.

zh: 软武术，例如太极拳，教你利用对手自身的力量来保护自己。
en: Soft martial arts, such as tai chi, teach you to use your opponent's own force to defend yourself.

zh: 软流派运用流畅的动作和时机来躲避、偏转和重新引导对手的攻击。
en: Soft styles use fluid movements and timing to avoid, deflect and redirect their opponent's attacks.

zh: 这些武术对防御者是‘软’的，但对攻击者则不然！
en: These martial arts are soft for the defender but not for the attacker!

zh: 另一方面，硬流派，例如功夫，教你使用力量来保护自己。
en: On the other hand, hard styles, such as kung fu, teach you to defend yourself using force.

zh: 其技术包括格挡、摆姿势以及有力的出拳和踢腿。
en: Techniques involve blocking, posturing and powerful punching and kicking.

zh: 如果你的技巧更高超、力量更强大、速度比对手更快，那么硬派武术技术会更有效。
en: Hard martial arts techniques are more effective if you are more skilful, more powerful and faster than your opponent.

zh: 也许当今最著名的日本武术之一是空手道。
en: Perhaps one of the best known Japanese martial arts today is karate.

zh: 但你知道它的根源来自福建省吗？
en: But did you know that its roots are derived from Fujian Province?

zh: 14世纪时，一个来自福建的中国移民社区在当时还是琉球王国的地方定居下来。
en: In the 14th century, a community of Chinese migrants from Fujian settled in what was then the kingdom of Okinawa.

zh: 这些中国移民过去常常聚集在一个公园里享受文化活动——其中之一就是功夫。
en: The Chinese migrants used to gather in a park to enjoy cultural activities — one of which was kung fu.

zh: 这很快就引起了当地年轻人的注意，他们开始向他们的中国邻居学习功夫。
en: This soon caught the attention of local youths, who started learning kung fu from their Chinese neighbours.

zh: 事实上，中国对武术的影响并不局限于功夫。
en: In fact, Chinese influence in martial arts was not limited to kung fu.

zh: 第一本朝鲜军事训练手册就是基于一个中文版本编写的。
en: The very first Korean military training manual was based on a Chinese version.

zh: 在16世纪日本入侵朝鲜期间，朝鲜军队需要一种有效的方法来训练大量军队。
en: During the Japanese invasion of Korea in the 16th century, the Korean army needed an effective way to train a large number of troops.

zh: 他们采用了一种来自中国军事手册《纪效新书》的训练方法，该书由著名的中国将领戚继光所著，他曾击败过日本海盗。
en: They adopted a training methodology from a Chinese military manual called Ji Xiao Xin Shu, written by the famed Chinese general, Qi Jiguang, who had himself defeated Japanese pirates.

zh: 享受乐趣并保持健康！锻炼你的身体和心灵！
en: Have fun and get fit! Exercise your body and mind!

zh: 喜欢跳舞和派对吗？拉丁舞适合你——它将美妙的音乐和舞蹈与高强度锻炼（每小时燃烧1000卡路里）结合在一起。
en: Love dancing and parties? Latin dance is for you—it combines great music and dancing with hard exercise (burn 1,000 calories an hour).

zh: 一位经验丰富的拉丁舞教练，她的课程非常受欢迎。
en: An experienced Latin dance trainer and her classes are very popular.

zh: 瑜伽课程非常适合想要提高灵活性和力量的人。
en: Yoga classes are perfect for people who want to improve flexibility and strength.

zh: 一位经验丰富的老师，她接受过多种不同瑜伽风格的培训。
en: An experienced teacher who has trained in several different styles of yoga.

zh: 所有设备都提供，只需带一条毛巾和一些水。
en: All equipment is provided, just bring a towel and some water.

zh: 课程仅适合有经验的学习者。
en: Classes are only suitable for experienced learners.

zh: 动感单车课程是一种安全有效的锻炼方式——无论你体重多少、年龄多大或身体状况如何。
en: Spin classes are a safe and effective way to work out — it doesn’t matter how much you weigh, how old you are, or how fit you are.

zh: 你决定自己想努力的程度！
en: You decide how hard you want to work!

zh: 每节动感单车课程都是一次很好的心脏强化锻炼——它能快速燃烧脂肪！
en: Every spin class is a great cardio workout — it burns fat fast!

zh: 它非常适合初学者或正在从伤病中恢复的人，因为它的冲击力低——这意味着它不会伤害你的关节。
en: It's great for beginners or people recovering from an injury because of its low impact — that means it won't hurt your joints.

zh: Jenny是一位有专业资格的动感单车教练，她会激励你在每节课中尽力而为。
en: Jenny is a certified spin instructor who'll inspire you to do your best in every class.

zh: 我最喜欢的体育明星是一位网球运动员。
en: My favourite sports star is a tennis player.

zh: 她充满活力且技术娴熟。
en: She is energetic and skilful.

zh: 她在很小的时候就开始学习网球。
en: She started learning tennis at a very young age.

zh: 那时，她没有意识到网球将成为她余生的热情所在。
en: At that time, she didn't realise that tennis would be her passion for the rest of her life.

zh: 她是一位有天赋的网球运动员。
en: She is a talented tennis player.

zh: 她赢得了许多奖项。
en: She has won many awards.

zh: 她在2008年北京奥运会上获得了第四名。
en: She came fourth at the 2008 Beijing Olympic Games.

zh: 2011年对她来说是非常重要的一年。
en: 2011 was a very important year for her.

zh: 那一年，她不仅在澳大利亚网球公开赛上赢得了银牌，还在法国网球公开赛上赢得了金牌。
en: That year, she not only won the silver medal in the Australian Open but also won the gold medal in the French Open.

zh: 它们都是世界级的锦标赛。
en: They are both world-class tournaments.

zh: 她是一位优秀的运动员。
en: She is an excellent player.

zh: 她的粉丝们都支持她并祝愿她一切顺利。
en: Her fans all support her and wish her the best.

zh: 是的，她就是李娜。
en: Yes, she is Li Na.

zh: 爆竹声中一岁除，春风送暖入屠苏。
en: Amid the boom of firecrackers a year has come to an end, and the spring wind has wafted warm breath to the wine.

zh: 元旦是每个人的生日。
en: New Year's Day is every man's birthday.

zh: 与你的恶习作战，与你的邻居和睦相处，让每一个新年都让你成为一个更好的人。
en: Be at war with your vices, at peace with your neighbours, and let every new year find you a better man.

zh: 圣诞节不是一个季节。它是一种感觉。
en: Christmas isn't a season. It's a feeling.

zh: 这是我第一次和我的寄宿家庭在中国过春节。
en: It was my first time spending Spring Festival in China with my host family.

zh: 春节受到每个中国家庭的高度重视。
en: Spring Festival is highly valued by every Chinese family.

zh: 准备工作在节日前一周就开始了。
en: Preparations began a week before the festival.

zh: 首先，房子被从上到下彻底打扫了一遍。
en: First, the house was cleaned from top to bottom.

zh: 我的寄宿家庭母亲陈太太说，这是为了扫除过去一年的污垢，为新年做好准备。
en: My host mother Mrs Chen said this was to sweep away the dirt of the past year and get ready for the new year.

zh: 接着，汉字“福”被倒着贴在了我们的前门上。
en: Next, the Chinese character Fu was attached upside down to our front door.

zh: 人们相信，当“福”字倒着贴时，幸福就会到来。
en: It is believed that when Fu is put upside down, happiness arrives.

zh: 这个字看起来真的很酷。
en: The character looked really cool.

zh: 最大的兴奋感在春节前夜开始了。
en: The greatest excitement began the night before Spring Festival.

zh: 烟花在整个城市燃放，突然照亮了夜空。
en: Fireworks were being let off across the city, suddenly lighting up the night sky.

zh: 孩子们捂着耳朵，但脸上带着期待的表情。
en: Children were covering their ears but with an expectant look on their faces.

zh: 陈太太说这是为了吓跑年兽。
en: Mrs Chen said that it was to scare away the monster Nian.

zh: 烟花非常有趣！
en: The fireworks were a lot of fun!

zh: 然后我们坐在一起享用丰盛的晚餐，祝愿大家新年快乐。
en: We then sat together to enjoy a big dinner, wishing everyone a happy new year.

zh: 我通常会在春节前几周就开始计划回山西的行程，因为火车票或机票必须尽早预订。
en: I usually start planning my trip home to Shanxi weeks before Spring Festival, as train tickets or flights have to be booked as early as possible.

zh: 我非常细心地为父母挑选礼物。
en: I take great care in choosing gifts for my parents.

zh: 今年，我给父亲买了一瓶酒，给母亲买了一件漂亮的毛衣。
en: This year, I bought a bottle of wine for my father and a beautiful sweater for my mother.

zh: 每年，我一上火车，就被山西口音包围——我知道我正在回家的路上。
en: Every year, the moment I get on the train, I am surrounded by Shanxi accents — I know that I am heading home to my family.

zh: 我不常回山西，所以当我回到家时，父母会告诉我发生的事情——谁结婚了，谁有了孩子，或者谁去上大学了。
en: I don't get to travel back to Shanxi very often, so when I get home, my parents will fill me in on what's been happening — who has got married or had children or gone away to university.

zh: 听到大家的近况真是太好了。
en: It’s great to hear what everyone's been up to.

zh: 我们通常吃火锅——非常美味！
en: We usually have hotpot — it’s so tasty!

zh: 然而，我们吃什么并不是最重要的事情。
en: However, what we eat isn’t the most important thing.

zh: 重要的是和谁一起吃。
en: What's important is who we eat it with.

zh: 回到家人身边，谈论往事，是一件美妙的事情。
en: It’s a wonderful thing to be back together with my family and talk of old times.

zh: 当我还是个孩子的时候，春节就是放鞭炮、吃糖果和装饰品。
en: When I was a kid, Spring Festival was all about firecrackers, sweets and decorations.

zh: 随着年龄的增长，回家和全家人在一起是最重要的部分。
en: As I get older, coming home and being with my entire family is the most important part of it.

zh: 春节是我们的家庭时间。
en: Spring Festival is family time for us.

zh: 我和我的丈夫都退休了。
en: My husband and I are both retired.

zh: 我们不常见到我们的孩子和孙子，非常想念他们。
en: We do not see our children and grandchildren very often and we miss them a lot.

zh: 春节前几周，我们就开始为他们的归来做准备。
en: Weeks before Spring Festival, we start getting ready for their homecoming.

zh: 事情开始变得非常忙碌。
en: Things start to get really busy.

zh: 我们考虑孩子们喜欢吃什么，我们需要买什么，以及需要准备什么菜肴。
en: We think about what the children would like to eat, what we need to buy and what dishes need to be prepared.

zh: 当每个人都回家时，我们的房子就充满了活动的嗡嗡声。
en: Our house buzzes with activities when everyone comes home.

zh: 我们的孙子们跑来跑去，喊叫着玩耍，大人们围坐在桌子旁谈论过去的一年。
en: Our grandchildren run around shouting and playing, and the adults gather around the table to talk about the past year.

zh: 在包饺子的时候，厨房里也充满了乐趣。
en: Great fun is also enjoyed in the kitchen as jiaozi are being made.

zh: 我们在这个时候总是吃饺子，因为它们在这个场合有特殊的含义——它们象征着我们对来年健康和幸福的祝愿。
en: We always have jiaozi during this time, as they mean something special on this occasion — they are a sign of our wishes for health and happiness in the coming year.

zh: 对我来说，这就是春节——家庭团聚的快乐。
en: To me, this is Spring Festival — the joy of a family gathering.

zh: 纵观历史，一些原始的习俗已经改变，但节日的精神——家庭的精神——在很大程度上得以保留。
en: Throughout history, some of the original customs have changed, but the spirit of the festival — the spirit of family — has largely been kept.

zh: 大多数重要的春节传统仍然在全国各地的人们中实践着。
en: Most of the important Spring Festival traditions are still being practised by people across the country.

zh: 我仍然记得奶奶和我们一起度过的最后一个圣诞节。它开始于十一月中的一个寒冷、刮风的日子。
en: I still remember the last Christmas Granny spent with us. It began in the middle of November on a cold, windy day.

zh: 我记得那风，因为奶奶到达时，她的灰白头发乱糟糟的。
en: I remember the wind because Granny's grey hair was a mess when she arrived.

zh: 那天也是奶奶搬来和我们同住的日子。
en: That was also the day Granny moved in.

zh: 不知何故，当时我不知道她生病了。
en: Somehow, at the time, I didn’t know she was sick.

zh: 也许我太年轻，也许她隐藏得太好，或者也许我只是因为圣诞节即将到来而太兴奋。
en: Maybe I was too young, maybe she hid it too well, or maybe I was just too excited that Christmas was on the way.

zh: 奶奶搬进来后，她大部分时间都待在火炉旁的棕色沙发上。
en: After Granny moved in, she spent most of her time on the brown sofa by the fire.

zh: 有时她很安静，但她似乎很开心。
en: Sometimes she was quiet, but she seemed happy.

zh: 当我给圣诞老人写信告诉他我想要的礼物时，奶奶努力帮助我。
en: When I wrote a letter to Father Christmas telling him about the presents I wanted, Granny made an effort to help me.

zh: 她的手微微颤抖，但她在信封上写下了“圣诞老人，北极”。
en: Her hand was slightly shaky, but she wrote “Father Christmas, the North Pole” on the envelope.

zh: 十二月，我们的兴奋与日俱增——街上亮起了灯，圣诞卡到了，到处都是雪。
en: In December, our excitement grew each day — lights appeared in the streets, Christmas cards arrived, and there was snow everywhere.

zh: 奶奶和我做了糖饼干，给火鸡填了馅，然后我坐在奶奶的腿上，我们一起唱圣诞歌曲。
en: Granny and I made sugar biscuits, stuffed the turkey and then I sat on my granny’s lap and we sang Christmas songs.

zh: 我最喜欢的歌是《雪人弗罗斯蒂》，我喜欢那句“砰-砰-砰，砰-砰-砰，看弗罗斯蒂走！”
en: My favourite song was Frosty the Snowman and I loved the bit that went “Thumpety-thump-thump, thumpety-thump-thump, look at Frosty go!”

zh: 奶奶有耐心一遍又一遍地唱它。
en: Granny had the patience to sing it over and over again.

zh: 平安夜，当我们布置圣诞树时，奶奶在火炉边坐了下来。
en: On Christmas Eve, Granny took a seat by the fire as we put up the Christmas tree.

zh: 我把大部分的装饰品都挂在了树枝上。
en: I hung most of the decorations on the branches.

zh: “这是我见过最漂亮的树，”奶奶说。
en: “That's the most beautiful tree I've ever seen,” Granny said.

zh: 然后她开始唱：“哦，圣诞树，哦，圣诞树，你让我的心中充满音乐……”
en: Then she started singing, “Oh, Christmas tree, oh, Christmas tree, you fill my heart with music ...”

zh: 睡前，奶奶和我留了一盘糖饼干给圣诞老人，并在我的床尾放了一只长袜。
en: Before bed, Granny and I left a plate of sugar biscuits out for Father Christmas, and put a stocking at the end of my bed.

zh: 我记得奶奶坐在我的床上轻声唱歌，直到我睡着。
en: I remember Granny sitting on my bed and singing softly until I fell asleep.

zh: 圣诞早晨，我的长袜里装满了小玩具和糖果，树下还有礼物。
en: My stocking was full of small toys and sweets on Christmas morning, and there were presents under the tree.

zh: 我们拆礼物时，我表现得特别好。
en: I was on my best behaviour as we opened the presents.

zh: 后来，奶奶叫我过去。
en: Later, Granny called me over.

zh: “我有特别的东西给你，”奶奶低声说。
en: “I have something special for you,” Granny whispered.

zh: “是给你装所有宝贝的。
en: “It's for all your treasures.

zh: 那是一个音乐盒，里面有一个芭蕾舞者。
en: It was a music box with a ballet dancer inside.

zh: 早餐后，凯丝阿姨和我的表弟大卫来了。
en: After breakfast, Auntie Kath and my cousin David arrived.

zh: 妈妈、爸爸和凯丝阿姨准备午餐时，大卫和我在外面玩。
en: While Mum, Dad and Auntie Kath got lunch ready, David and I played outside.

zh: 我们在花园里跑来跑去，滚了一个大雪球做了一个可爱的雪人。
en: We raced around the garden and rolled a big snowball to make a lovely snowman.

zh: 奶奶待在屋里烤火。
en: Granny stayed inside by the fire.

zh: 我记得透过窗户与她对视。
en: I remember catching her eye through the window.

zh: “出来吧，奶奶，”我喊道，但她只是挥了挥手。
en: “Come outside, Granny,” I called, but she just waved.

zh: 午餐吃得很晚，但气氛热闹又兴奋。
en: Lunch was late, but it was a loud and exciting affair.

zh: 我们戴上傻乎乎的纸帽子，吃了一大只火鸡配土豆，然后是圣诞布丁。
en: We put on silly paper hats and had a big turkey with potatoes, followed by Christmas pudding.

zh: 大卫往嘴里塞了太多布丁，以至于咽不下去。
en: David put so much pudding in his mouth that he couldn't swallow.

zh: “布丁怎么样？”奶奶问。
en: “How's the pudding?” Granny asked.

zh: “嗯，嗯，嗯，”大卫说。
en: “Mmmph, mmmph, mmmph,” said David.

zh: 凯丝阿姨觉得这不好笑，但我们其他人都觉得好笑。
en: Auntie Kath didn’t think it was funny, but the rest of us did.

zh: 奶奶笑得纸帽子都掉了下来。
en: Granny laughed so much that her paper hat fell off.

zh: 那个圣诞节几周后，奶奶去世了。
en: A few weeks after that Christmas, Granny passed away.

zh: 但我经常想起她，尤其是在圣诞节。
en: I think of her often though, especially at Christmas.

zh: 每年，我都会拿出她送给我的音乐盒。
en: Each year, I take out the music box she gave me.

zh: 里面保存着奶奶的纸帽子和那个圣诞节她帮我写的信。
en: Inside I keep Granny's paper hat and the letter she helped me write that Christmas.

zh: 几年前，当我不再相信圣诞老人时，妈妈把这封信还给了我。
en: Mum gave the letter back to me some years ago when I no longer believed in Father Christmas.

zh: 当我思考是什么让圣诞节如此神奇时，想到的不仅仅是礼物和圣诞老人。
en: When I think about what makes Christmas so magical, it’s not just gifts and Father Christmas that come to mind.

zh: 还有我对奶奶的记忆。
en: It’s also my memory of Granny.

zh: 现在每年做糖饼干的人是我，而且我总是会做一个G形的——G代表奶奶。
en: Now I'm the one who makes sugar biscuits each year, and I always make one in a G-shape — G for Granny.

zh: 那天是爷爷的70岁生日。
en: It was Grandpa's 70th birthday.

zh: 全家人已经为此准备了相当长一段时间。
en: The whole family had been preparing for it for quite some time.

zh: 一大早，我就兴奋地醒了。
en: Early in the morning, I woke up excited.

zh: 首先我迅速穿好衣服。
en: First I got dressed quickly.

zh: 然后我下楼去帮忙。
en: Then I headed downstairs to help.

zh: 妈妈已经在厨房准备爷爷的特殊生日午餐了：爸爸却不见踪影。
en: Mum was already in the kitchen preparing Grandpa's special birthday lunch: Dad was nowhere to be seen.

zh: 快速吃完早餐后，我最后检查了一遍我为爷爷制作的特别礼物——一部关于他人生故事的视频。
en: After a quick breakfast, I checked the special gift I had made for Grandpa, a video of his life story — for the last time.

zh: 几周来我一直在忙这个，听爷爷讲故事，挑选他最喜欢的音乐，并从家庭相册中收集照片。
en: For weeks I had been working on this, listening to Grandpa’s stories, selecting his favourite music and collecting photos from the family album.

zh: 就在午餐前，爸爸带着一些客人进来了。
en: Just before lunchtime, Dad came in with some guests.

zh: 令人惊讶的是，他们是爷爷中学时最好的朋友，现在都七十多岁了。
en: Surprisingly, they were Grandpa's best friends from secondary school, all in their seventies.

zh: 爷爷不敢相信自己的眼睛，他们花了很长时间才平静下来。
en: Grandpa couldn't believe his eyes and it took quite a long time for them to calm down.

zh: 客人们一就座，爷爷最喜欢的音乐就开始在房间里回荡，充满了对往昔的回忆。
en: As soon as the guests took their seats, Grandpa's favourite music began to fill the room with memories of the old days.

zh: 随着每一张老照片的播放，客人们看到了爷爷是如何从一个英俊的男孩成长为一位和蔼的老绅士的。
en: With each old photo, the guests saw how Grandpa grew from a handsome boy to a kind old gentleman.

zh: 最精彩的部分是当爷爷和他的朋友们穿着校服出现在一张老照片中时。
en: The best part came when Grandpa and his friends appeared in an old photo in their school uniforms.

zh: 房间里充满了笑声，他们的眼中也含着不少泪水。
en: There was a lot of laughter in the room, and quite a few tears in their eyes.

zh: 在派对接近尾声时，爷爷兴奋地说：“这是一个美妙的生日庆祝活动，我收到了我收到过的最好的礼物。
en: Towards the end of the party, Grandpa said excitedly, “It is a wonderful birthday celebration and I've got the best gift I have ever received.

zh: 现在我已经在期待我的八十岁、九十岁甚至一百岁生日了！”
en: Now I am looking forward to my eightieth, ninetieth and even one hundredth birthdays already!”

zh: 食物在中国的庆祝活动中扮演着重要角色。
en: Food plays a major role in celebrations in China.

zh: 春节菜肴充满了特殊的文化含义，被视为人们表达对来年希望和愿望的一种方式。
en: Spring Festival dishes are full of special cultural meanings, and are seen as a way for people to express their hopes and wishes for the coming year.

zh: 中文的“鱼”字与“余”（剩余）发音相同，表示食物和财富的丰足。
en: The Chinese word for fish is "鱼" which sounds the same as "余"(surplus), indicating plenty of food and wealth.

zh: 因此这道菜字面意思是食物有剩余，象征着财富充裕。
en: So the dish literally means a surplus of food and symbolises plentiful wealth.

zh: 中国饺子，也称为jiaozi（饺子），是用小片面团包裹馅料制成的。
en: Chinese dumplings, also called jiaozi (饺子), are small pieces of dough wrapped around a filling.

zh: 饺子在中文里的意思是“年岁的更替”。
en: Jiaozi in Chinese means "transition of years".

zh: 饺子的形状也类似于中国古代使用的货币，因此人们相信它们会带来财富和繁荣。
en: The shape of jiaozi is also similar to the currency used in ancient China, so it is believed that they will bring wealth and prosperity.

zh: 这种布丁中的所有八种原料都象征着好运和幸福。
en: All eight kinds of ingredients in this pudding symbolise good luck and happiness.

zh: 它看起来也像一个聚宝盆，可以聚集财富。
en: It also looks like a treasure bowl, which can gather a fortune.

zh: 这道菜中的四个肉丸象征着人生四大喜：福、禄、寿、喜。
en: The four meatballs in this dish symbolise four joys in life: blessing, wealth, longevity and happiness.

zh: 圆形的形状也是团聚的象征，代表着新年的幸福。
en: The round shape is also a symbol of gathering together, standing for happiness in the new year.

zh: 刘易斯·卡罗尔于1332年出生于英格兰，逝世于1398年。
en: Lewis Carroll was born in 1332 in England and died in 1398.

zh: 卡罗尔最著名的作品是《爱丽丝梦游仙境》（1865年出版）和《爱丽丝镜中奇遇记》（1871年出版）。
en: Carroll’s most famous books were Alice’s Adventures in Wonderland (published in 1865) and Through the Looking-Glass (published in 1871).

zh: 除了作家身份，他还是一位数学家和摄影师，同时还从事其他工作。
en: As well as being a writer, he was also a mathematician and photographer, amongst other things.

zh: 爱丽丝在这个世界里遇到了各种各样的生物，包括矮胖墩，一个看起来像鸡蛋的矮胖的人。
en: Alice met different kinds of creatures in this world, including Humpty Dumpty, a dumpy person who looks like an egg.

zh: “你系的这条腰带真漂亮！”爱丽丝突然说道。
en: “What a beautiful belt you've got on!” Alice suddenly said.

zh: “至少，”她纠正自己道，“一条漂亮的围巾，我本应这么说——不，我的意思是腰带——请您原谅！”
en: “At least,” she corrected herself, “a beautiful scarf, I should have said — no, a belt, I mean — I beg your pardon!”

zh: 矮胖墩看起来非常生气，她开始希望自己没有选择这个话题。
en: Humpty Dumpty looked thoroughly offended, and she began to wish she hadn't chosen that subject.

zh: “要是我知道就好了，”她心里想，“哪是脖子哪是腰！”
en: “If only I knew,” she thought to herself, “which was neck and which was waist!”

zh: 显然矮胖墩非常生气，尽管他一两分钟什么也没说。
en: Evidently Humpty Dumpty was very angry, though he said nothing for a minute or two.

zh: 当他再次开口时，声音是一种低沉的咆哮。
en: When he did speak again, it was in a deep growl.

zh: “这是一件——非常——恼人的——事情，”他终于说道，“当一个人分不清围巾和腰带时！”
en: “It is a —most— provoking — thing,” he said at last, “when a person doesn't know a scarf from a belt!”

zh: “我知道这显得我很无知，”爱丽丝说道，态度如此谦卑，以至于矮胖墩心软了。
en: “I know it's very ignorant of me,” Alice said, so humbly that Humpty Dumpty relented.

zh: “这是一条围巾，孩子，而且如你所说，是一条漂亮的围巾。
en: “It’s a scarf, child, and a beautiful one, as you say.

zh: 这是白国王和白王后送的礼物。
en: It’s a present from the White King and Queen.

zh: “真的吗？”爱丽丝说，很高兴地发现自己毕竟选了一个好话题。
en: “Is it really?” said Alice, quite pleased to find that she had chosen a good subject, after all.

zh: “他们把它给了我，”矮胖墩若有所思地继续说道，同时他将一条腿搭在另一条腿上，并用双手抱住它。
en: “They gave it to me,” Humpty Dumpty continued thoughtfully, as he crossed one knee over the other and wrapped his hands round it.

zh: 他们把它给了我——作为一份非生日礼物。”
en: They gave it to me — as an un-birthday present.”

zh: “请您再说一遍？”爱丽丝说。
en: “I beg your pardon?” Alice said.

zh: “我的意思是，什么是非生日礼物？”
en: “I mean, what is an un-birthday present?”

zh: “当然是在不是你生日的时候送的礼物，”矮胖墩说。
en: “A present given when it isn’t your birthday, of course,” Humpty Dumpty said.

zh: “我最喜欢生日礼物，”她最后说道。
en: “I like birthday presents best,” she said at last.

zh: “你根本不知道你在说什么！”矮胖墩喊道。
en: “You don’t know what you're talking about!” cried Humpty Dumpty.

zh: “一年有多少天？”
en: “How many days are there in a year?”

zh: “三百六十五天，”爱丽丝说。
en: “Three hundred and sixty-five,” said Alice.

zh: 你有多少个生日？
en: And how many birthdays have you?

zh: 如果你从三百六十五中减去一，剩下多少？
en: And if you take one from three hundred and sixty-five, what remains?

zh: 当然是三百六十四。
en: Three hundred and sixty-four, of course.

zh: 我宁愿在纸上看到这个计算，他说。
en: I'd rather see that done on paper, he said.

zh: 爱丽丝忍不住笑了，她拿出笔记本，为他计算了这个数字：
en: Alice couldn't help smiling as she took out her notebook, and worked the sum for him:

zh: 矮胖子接过笔记本，仔细地看着它。
en: Humpty Dumpty took the notebook, and looked at it carefully.

zh: 这看起来做对了——他开始说。
en: That seems to be done right — he began.

zh: 你把它拿反了！爱丽丝打断道。
en: You're holding it upside down! Alice interrupted.

zh: 确实是我拿反了！矮胖子高兴地说，这时她为他转了过来。
en: To be sure I was! Humpty Dumpty said happily, as she turned it round for him.

zh: 我以为它看起来有点奇怪。
en: I thought it looked a little odd.

zh: 正如我刚才说的，这看起来做对了——尽管我现在没有时间彻底检查——这表明有三百六十四天你可能会收到非生日礼物——
en: As I was saying, that seems to be done right — though I haven't time to look it over thoroughly right now — and that shows that there are three hundred and sixty-four days when you might get un-birthday presents —

zh: 而只有一天是生日礼物！
en: And only one for birthday presents!

zh: 春节是中国人民最重要的节日。
en: Spring Festival is the most important festival for people in China.

zh: 节前一周，许多家庭就准备好进行一次大扫除。
en: A week before the festival, many families get ready for to do a general house cleaning.

zh: 之后，他们开始悬挂红色剪纸的装饰品，以迎接新年并带来好运。
en: After that, they start to hang decorations with red coloured paper cuts, in order to get ready for the New Year and bring good luck.

zh: 人们还会张贴以“好运”和“财富”为主题的流行春联。
en: People also put up Spring Festival couplets (春联) with popular themes of “good fortune” and “wealth”.

zh: 从这一天起，节日的气氛随着越来越兴奋而逐渐浓厚。
en: From this day, the festival atmosphere builds with increasing excitement.

zh: 人们提前几周制定旅行计划，以确保他们能够在春节前夕回家与家人共进晚餐。
en: People make travel plans a few weeks in advance to ensure that they are able to return home to their family for dinner on the eve of Spring Festival.

zh: 春节前夜，所有家庭成员聚在一起，围坐在桌旁享用一顿丰盛的晚餐。
en: The night before Spring Festival, all family members get together to have a big dinner around a table.

zh: 大人们给孩子们分发装有压岁钱的小红包。
en: Adults give out little red envelopes to children containing money.

zh: 人们观看电视上的春节联欢晚会，唱歌，玩游戏，并熬夜到深夜。
en: People watch the Spring Festival gala on TV, sing songs, play games and stay up late in the night.

zh: 房子里充满了欢声笑语。
en: Houses are full of laughter.

zh: 春节也是努力并表达对祖先的尊敬的时候。
en: Spring Festival is also the time to make efforts and show respect to ancestors.

zh: 那是一个美妙的夜晚！
en: It was a wonderful night!

zh: 我太兴奋了，睡不着。
en: I'm too excited to fall asleep.

zh: 我的新同学大卫来自伦敦，他邀请我参加他的派对。
en: David, my new classmate from London invited me to his party.

zh: 我感到兴奋但也有点紧张，因为我不知道参加由英国人举办的派对的规则。
en: I felt excited but also a little nervous because I didn't know the rules for attending a party held by someone from England.

zh: 因此，在派对前几天，我开始准备。
en: Therefore, days before the party, I started to prepare.

zh: 我努力在网上寻找一些关于如何穿着得体、带什么东西以及如何与他人社交的建议。
en: I made an effort to find some tips online about how to dress appropriately, what things to bring and also how to socialise with others.

zh: 一些建议真的很有帮助。
en: Some advice was really helpful.

zh: 因为这是一个非正式的派对，我穿了一件T恤和牛仔裤。
en: As it was an informal party, I wore a T-shirt and jeans.

zh: 我还准备了食物和零食，并把它们带到了派对上。
en: I also prepare food and snacks and took them to the party.

zh: 令人高兴的是，人们非常喜欢我带来的食物。
en: Happily, people enjoyed the food I brought very much.

zh: 当我走进大卫家时，首先吸引我眼球的是用许多彩色气球做成的“欢迎”字样。
en: When I entered David's house, what catch my eye first was the word “Welcome”, made from many colourful balloons.

zh: 大卫和他的朋友们还在墙上张贴了装饰品。
en: David and his friends also put up decorations on the wall.

zh: 大卫非常友善，请我坐下。
en: David was very kind and asked me to take a seat.

zh: 我在那里认识许多其他交换生。
en: I knew many other exchange students there.

zh: 他们告诉我许多关于伦敦的有趣事情。
en: They told me lots of interesting things about London.

zh: 我介绍了许多中国著名的地方和食物。
en: I introduced many famous places and food in China.

zh: 我们晚餐吃了披萨。
en: We had pizza for dinner.

zh: 晚餐后，大家聚在一起玩了一个纸牌游戏。
en: After dinner, everyone get together and played a card game.

zh: 这是我参加过的最好的派对之一。
en: It was one of the best parties I have ever attended.

zh: 埃比尼泽·斯克鲁奇在他的办公室里，斯克鲁奇和马利的办公室。
en: Ebenezer Scrooge was in his office, the office of Scrooge and Marley.

zh: 他的职员，可怜的鲍勃·克拉奇正在工作，这时斯克鲁奇的侄子进来了。
en: His clerk, poor Bob Cratchit, was working, when Scrooge’s nephew came in.

zh: “你好，叔叔。圣诞快乐！”他说。
en: “Hello Uncle. Merry Christmas!” he said.

zh: “你明天想和我们一起吃圣诞晚餐吗？”
en: “Do you want to have Christmas dinner with us tomorrow?”

zh: “圣诞节？”斯克鲁奇回答。“呸！骗人的玩意儿！”
en: “Christmas?” Scrooge replied. “Bah! Humbug!”

zh: 斯克鲁奇讨厌圣诞节，所以他拒绝了他侄子的邀请。
en: Scrooge hated Christmas, so he refused his nephew's invitation.

zh: 后来有两个人来到办公室，为穷人要钱。
en: Later two men came to the office, asking for money for the poor.

zh: “呸！难道没有监狱给这些人吗？”
en: “Bah! Are there no prisons for these people?”

zh: 斯克鲁奇连一分钱都不肯给。
en: Scrooge refused to give even a penny.

zh: 到了关门的时候，鲍勃·克拉奇请求圣诞节放假。
en: When it was time to close the office, Bob Cratchit asked for Christmas day off.

zh: “好吧，”斯克鲁奇说，“但第二天早上要早点到！”
en: “All right,” Scrooge said, “but be here early the next morning!”

zh: 那天晚上，斯克鲁奇正坐在家里的火炉旁，一个幽灵出现了。
en: That evening, Scrooge was sitting by the fire at home when a ghost appeared.

zh: “你是谁？”斯克鲁奇紧张地问。
en: “Who are you?” Scrooge asked nervously.

zh: “雅各布·马利，你的合伙人。我戴着这些锁链，永远无法安宁，因为我活着的时候，只想着钱。
en: “Jacob Marley, your partner. I am wearing these chains and can never be in peace, because when I lived, I only thought about money.

zh: 但我来这里是为了帮助你。
en: But I am here to help you.

zh: 你有机会逃脱我可怕的命运。
en: You have a chance to escape my terrible destiny.

zh: 今晚三个幽灵将会拜访你。”
en: Tonight three ghosts will visit you.”

zh: 然后马利的幽灵消失了。
en: Then the ghost of Marley disappeared.

zh: 斯克鲁奇上床睡觉了。
en: Scrooge went to bed and fell asleep.

zh: 但在夜里，他醒了。
en: But in the night, he woke up.

zh: 一个奇怪老人的身影就在他的床边。
en: The figure of a strange old man was near his bed.

zh: “我是过去的圣诞幽灵。你的过去，”它告诉斯克鲁奇。
en: “I am the Ghost of Christmas Past. Of your past,” it told Scrooge.

zh: 幽灵带斯克鲁奇去看过去圣诞节的场景。
en: The ghost took Scrooge to scenes of Christmases from the past.

zh: 在一个场景中，斯克鲁奇看到自己还是个在学校读书的男孩。
en: In one scene, Scrooge saw himself as a boy at school.

zh: 所有其他男孩都回家过圣诞节了。
en: All the other boys had gone home for Christmas.

zh: 在另一个场景中，斯克鲁奇看到自己是个年轻人。
en: In another, Scrooge saw himself as a young man.

zh: 他正在和他的女朋友说话，他没有娶她是因为她穷。
en: He was talking to his girlfriend, who he didn't marry because she was poor.

zh: 斯克鲁奇感到越来越难过。
en: Scrooge felt sadder and sadder.

zh: “停下！别再给我看了！”他喊道。
en: “Stop! Show me no more!” he cried.

zh: 幽灵把他带回家，斯克鲁奇又睡着了。
en: The ghost brought him home and Scrooge fell asleep again.

zh: 那天晚上晚些时候，他又醒了。
en: Later that night, he woke up again.

zh: “我是现在的圣诞幽灵。看着我！”第二个幽灵笑着说。
en: “I am the Ghost of Christmas Present. Look at me!” said the second ghost, laughing.

zh: 他是一个穿着绿色长袍的高大男人。
en: He was a large man wearing a green robe.

zh: 他带斯克鲁奇去了鲍勃·克拉奇的家。
en: He took Scrooge to the house of Bob Cratchit.

zh: 房子里很冷，鲍勃和他的家人正围着一个非常小的圣诞布丁坐着。
en: The house was cold and Bob and his family were sitting around a very small Christmas pudding.

zh: "多么美味的布丁啊。大家圣诞快乐！"鲍勃说。
en: "What a wonderful pudding. Merry Christmas everyone!" said Bob.

zh: 斯克鲁奇感到难过，因为他能看到克拉特基特一家是多么贫穷。
en: Scrooge felt bad, because he could see how poor the Cratchits were.

zh: 鲍勃最小的孩子小蒂姆体弱多病。
en: Bob's youngest child, Tiny Tim, was weak and ill.

zh: 幽灵最终把斯克鲁奇带到了街上。
en: The ghost finally took Scrooge out into the street.

zh: 有两个可怜的孩子在外面受冻。
en: There were two poor children out in the cold.

zh: "我们不能做点什么来帮助这些孩子吗？"他问幽灵，幽灵重复了斯克鲁奇的话。
en: "Can't we do something to help these children?" he asked the ghost, who repeated Scrooge's words.

zh: "难道没有监狱吗？"幽灵大笑着消失了。
en: "Are there no prisons?" The ghost laughed and disappeared.

zh: 然后，第三个幽灵出现了。
en: Then, the third ghost appeared.

zh: 他身穿黑衣。
en: He was dressed in black.

zh: "你是未来圣诞幽灵吗？"斯克鲁奇问道。
en: "Are you the Ghost of Christmas Future?" Scrooge asked.

zh: 幽灵没有回答。
en: The ghost did not answer.

zh: 它带着斯克鲁奇，向他展示了未来的场景。
en: It took Scrooge and showed him scenes of the future.

zh: 在一个场景中，人们谈论着斯克鲁奇的死，但没有人感到难过。
en: In one, people talked about Scrooge’s death, but nobody was unhappy.

zh: 幽灵还带他去了克拉特基特家。
en: The ghost also took him to the Cratchit family.

zh: 这家人非常悲伤，因为小男孩小蒂姆已经死了。
en: The family was very sad, because the little boy, Tiny Tim, had died.

zh: 第二天早上，斯克鲁奇打开窗户问道："今天是哪一天？"
en: The next morning, Scrooge opened his window and asked, "What day is it today?"

zh: "怎么了先生？今天是圣诞节，"街上的一个男孩回答。
en: "Why sir? It’s Christmas Day," replied a boy in the street.

zh: 他给那个男孩钱，让他为克拉特基特家买一只巨大的火鸡。
en: He gave money to the boy to buy an enormous turkey for the Cratchit family.

zh: "圣诞快乐！圣诞快乐！"他祝他看到的每一个人圣诞快乐。
en: "Merry Christmas! Merry Christmas!" He wished everybody he saw Merry Christmas.

zh: 他遇到了前一天来他办公室的那两个人，并给了他们一大笔钱来帮助穷人。
en: He met the two men who had come to his office the day before and gave them a large sum of money for the poor.

zh: 然后他去拜访了他的侄子，并留下来吃了圣诞晚餐。
en: Then he visited his nephew and stayed for Christmas dinner.

zh: 第二天他很早就去办公室等鲍勃·克拉特基特。
en: The next day he went to his office early to wait for Bob Cratchit.

zh: "你迟到了！"斯克鲁奇生气地说。
en: "You are late!" said Scrooge angrily.

zh: "是的，我非常抱歉……"可怜的鲍勃回答。
en: "Yes, I'm very sorry ..." replied poor Bob.

zh: "那样的话，恐怕我要……给你加薪！圣诞快乐，鲍勃！"
en: "In that case, I'm afraid I'm going to ... increase your salary! Merry Christmas, Bob!"

zh: 从那天起，斯克鲁奇变了。
en: From that day on, Scrooge was changed.

zh: 他捐钱给穷人。
en: He gave money to the poor.

zh: 他帮助了鲍勃·克拉特基特一家。
en: He helped Bob Cratchits family.

zh: 人们总是说："他知道如何庆祝圣诞节。"
en: And people always said, "He knew how to celebrate Christmas."

zh: 每个人有时都会感到压力。
en: Everyone can be stressed at times.

zh: 例如，许多学生可能会觉得考试压力很大。
en: For example, many students may find examinations stressful.

zh: 这里有一些给你的建议。
en: Here is some advice for you.

zh: 首先，不要给自己太大压力。
en: First, do not put too much pressure on yourself.

zh: 其次，设定可实现的目标，并用时间表来组织你的学习和工作。
en: Second, set achievable goals and organise your studies and work with a schedule.

zh: 告诉自己：“我可以做得更好！”
en: Tell yourself, “I can be better!”

zh: 做一些有挑战性的事情，你会感到兴奋。
en: Do something challenging and you will feel excited.

zh: 它可以帮助你减轻压力，也是一个富有成效且令人满意的爱好。
en: It can help you feel less stressed and it is also a fruitful and satisfying hobby.

zh: 一部威尔士的电视剧因其成为青少年的首选观看节目而令评论家们震惊。
en: A TV series in Wales has astounded critics by becoming the number one choice for teenagers to watch.

zh: 主角是一位年轻的农民，这部剧是关于乡村生活的，这使得该剧的流行更加令人惊讶。
en: The main character is a young farmer and the series is about country life, making the popularity of the series all the more surprising.

zh: 这部剧平静而轻松，与城市生活截然相反。
en: The series is calm and relaxed and really the opposite to city life.

zh: 《逃往新生活》将威尔士乡村描绘成一个美丽而宁静的地方，那里的人们非常关爱他人且幸福快乐。
en: Escape to a New Life portrays the Welsh countryside as a beautiful and peaceful place and its people as very caring and happy.

zh: 该剧导演认为，这就是当今年轻人想要的。
en: The series director believes that this is what young people want today.

zh: 她说这部剧的成功是因为青少年想要一种更快乐、更健康的生活，远离截止日期和考试的压力。
en: She says the success of the series is because teenagers want a happier and healthier life away from the pressures of deadlines and exams.

zh: 她认为这部剧提供了一种从他们快节奏和疯狂生活中逃离的形式。
en: She reckons this series offers a form of escape from their fast and frantic lives.

zh: 尽管如此，许多家长和老师担心这对年轻人产生的影响。
en: Despite this, many parents and teachers are worried about the effect this is having on young people.

zh: 家长们报告说，他们的孩子只顾着看这部剧而忽略了其他一切，用《逃往新生活》作为不完成作业或拒绝复习考试的借口，并声称需要一种更快乐、压力更小的生活。
en: Parents report that their children are just watching this series and neglecting everything else, using Escape to a New Life as an excuse for not completing homework, or refusing to revise for exams, citing a need for a happier and less stressful life.

zh: 一位名叫保罗·麦格雷戈的父亲说，他的女儿夏洛特改变了很多。
en: One father, Paul McGregor, said his daughter Charlotte had changed a lot.

zh: 他说：“她过去是学校田径队的，热爱标枪，但现在她只想看电视剧，并且已经被队里除名了。”
en: He said, "She used to be in the school athletics team and loved the javelin, but now she only wants to watch the TV series and has been dropped from the team.

zh: 她不再完成作业，说这没关系，因为她只想‘逃往新生活’。”
en: She no longer completes her homework saying it doesn't matter as she just wants to 'escape to a new life'."

zh: 专门研究儿童心理健康的儿科医生艾丽莎·卡哈特博士说，电视可以影响年轻人的人生决定，但她认为，如果一个年轻人的反应像夏洛特那样强烈，那么很可能还有其他原因导致她行为的改变。
en: Paediatrician, Dr. Elisa Carhart who specialises in child mental health, says that TV can influence young people's decisions in life, but believes that if a young person reacts as strongly as Charlotte, it's likely there are other reasons for the change in her behaviour.

zh: 我们的学校顾问张女士也是一名医生，她对运动的好处充满热情。
en: Mrs Zhang, our school counsellor who is also a medical doctor, is passionate about the advantages of playing sport.

zh: 她认为青少年拥有积极的态度至关重要，而运动能帮助他们做到这一点。
en: She believes that it is vital for teenagers to have a positive attitude, and sports help them do this.

zh: 她给了我们以下这个例子作为启发。
en: She gave us the following example as inspiration.

zh: 我曾有一位青少年病人，她情绪低落且喜怒无常。
en: I had a teenage patient, who was depressed and moody.

zh: 传统的做法认为我应该开某种药物。
en: Conventional wisdom said that I should prescribe medication of some sort.

zh: 我决定转而询问她的生活方式。
en: I decided rather to ask about her lifestyle.

zh: 从她的回答中可以清楚地看出她没有进行任何锻炼。
en: From her responses it was clear she was getting no exercise.

zh: 所以我鼓励她选择一项学校的体育活动并参与其中。
en: So I encouraged her to choose a school sporting activity and take part in it.

zh: 我建议她记录下所有新活动，以及她对这些活动和她自己的感受。
en: I suggested that she keep a journal of all her new activities, and how she felt about them and about herself.

zh: 珊接受了我的建议，加入了学校排球队。
en: Shan took my recommendation and joined the school volleyball team.

zh: 六个月后，她几乎让人认不出来了：她体重减轻了，看起来健康又健美；她有了更多朋友；她的学业进步了，最重要的是，她不再抑郁或喜怒无常了。
en: Six months later she was hardly recognisable: she had lost weight and was looking fit and healthy; she had more friends; her schoolwork had improved, and best of all, she was no longer depressed or moody.

zh: 她妈妈告诉我她像变了一个人。
en: Her mum told me she was a changed person.

zh: 珊后来告诉我，我改变了她的生活。
en: Shan told me later that I had changed her life.

zh: 是珊自己做到的。
en: It was Shan herself who did it.

zh: 她接受了我给她的想法，并以享受和决心去拥抱它。
en: She took the idea I gave her and embraced it with enjoyment and determination.

zh: 那是一场多么美妙的婚礼啊！
en: What a wonderful wedding that was!

zh: 这真是值得铭记的一天。
en: It was truly a day to remember.

zh: 我们享受其中的每一分钟。
en: We loved every minute of it.

zh: 你究竟是在哪里找到这么漂亮的婚纱的？
en: Where on earth did you find such a fantastic wedding dress?

zh: 而且我非常喜欢你做的发型。
en: And I loved the way you had done your hair.

zh: 詹姆斯是个幸运的男人。
en: James is a lucky man.

zh: 不过说真的，当你们一起进入宴会厅时，他看起来也非常英俊。
en: Mind you, he looked pretty handsome himself as you entered the reception together.

zh: 最精彩的环节之一是致辞。
en: One of the highlights was the speeches.

zh: 伴郎罗里非常风趣。
en: Rory, the best man, was so funny.

zh: 赵彤和我笑得眼泪都流下来了。
en: Zhao Tong and I laughed until tears ran down our faces.

zh: 就在那时，我看向我们旁边的桌子，看到了你的姑姑，你知道的，就是那个特地从澳大利亚赶来参加婚礼的那位。
en: Just then, I looked across at the table next to us and there was your aunt, you know the one that came all the way from Australia for the wedding.

zh: 她在桌子上睡着了。
en: She had fallen asleep at the table.

zh: 对她来说，这趟飞行一定很漫长！
en: It must have been a long flight for her!

zh: 这让我们笑得更厉害了。
en: That made us laugh even harder.

zh: 我非常担心我的睫毛膏会流到脸颊上，之后我不得不赶紧跑到洗手间去照镜子检查！
en: I was so worried that I had mascara running down my cheeks, I had to rush to the bathroom afterwards to check in the mirror!

zh: 我从未尝过这么美味的食物——而且摆盘如此精美。
en: I have never tasted such great food — and so beautifully served.

zh: 开胃菜八宝鸡非常美味，之后的主菜炸虾和米饭也同样好吃。
en: The starter of Eight Treasure Chicken was delicious, as was the main meal of fried prawns and rice which came afterwards.

zh: 甜点也非常可口。
en: The desserts were also scrumptious.

zh: 乐队很棒，演奏了美妙的歌曲。
en: The band was great and played fantastic songs.

zh: 音乐让所有人整晚都待在舞池里。
en: The music had everyone on the dance floor the whole night.

zh: 我不得不承认，当你们启程去度蜜月，我们向你和詹姆斯告别时，我眼里含着泪水。
en: I have to admit that I had tears in my eyes as we said goodbye to you and James as you left on your honeymoon.

zh: 我等不及想听你回来后讲述你的上海之行了。
en: I can’t wait to hear about your visit to Shanghai when you get back.

zh: 期待你回来后与你共度时光。
en: Looking forward to spending time with you when you get back.

zh: 时不时亲自与朋友见面很重要，而不仅仅是在社交媒体上。
en: It's important to meet friends in person from time to time, not just on social media.

zh: 我的目标是为我在大学的生物学学位做准备。
en: My target is to prepare myself for my degree in biology at university.

zh: 我努力保持阅读清单的更新。
en: I try to keep the reading list updated.

zh: 放学后我有更多的空闲时间去做其他我感兴趣的事情。
en: I have more free time to do other things that I'm interested in after school.

zh: 我已经做了足够多来鼓励人们相信我们下次能赢得比赛。
en: I have done enough to encourage people to believe we could win the game next time.

zh: 为了获得学位，她需要通过所有的考试。
en: To obtain the degree, she will need to pass all of the examinations.

zh: 你能告诉我如何去机场吗？
en: Could you tell me how to get to the airport?

zh: 你决定好面试穿什么了吗？
en: Have you decided what to wear to the interview?

zh: 我知道当我遇到麻烦时该向谁求助。
en: I know who to turn to for help when I'm in trouble.

zh: 她似乎没有注意到我。
en: She seemed not to notice me.

zh: 我们调低了音乐音量，以免打扰邻居。
en: We turned down the music in order not to disturb the neighbours.

zh: 漫长的一天过后，张天终于回到了他的小房间，感到疲惫。
en: After a long day, Zhang Tian finally got back to his small room, feeling tired.

zh: 每个人都对这场音乐会感到兴奋。
en: Everyone was excited about the concert.

zh: 他想象了关于在村庄生活和教学的各种令人兴奋的事情。
en: He imagined all sorts of exciting things about living and teaching in a village.

zh: 住在村子里是具有挑战性的。
en: Living in the village was challenging.

zh: 定语从句用来修饰、限定正在谈论的人或物。
en: We use relative clauses to identify the person or thing we are talking about.

zh: 当我们不在我们大楼旁边的球场上打球时，我们就在电视上看比赛。
en: When we weren't playing on the court which was next to our building, we were watching a game on TV.

zh: 保罗最喜欢的球员是泰龙·博格斯，一个为夏洛特黄蜂队效力的家伙。
en: Paul's favourite player was Tyrone Bogues, a guy who played for the Charlotte Hornets.

zh: 熊队，一支本赛季记录完美的球队。
en: The Bears, a team whose record this season had been perfect.

zh: 他不知道他很快就会得到他一直等待的机会。
en: He didn't know he'd soon get the chance that he'd been waiting for.

zh: 没有定语从句，就不知道所谈的是何人、何物。
en: Without the relative clause, we wouldn't know who, which, etc. we are referring to.

zh: 下列关系代词用在定语从句中:
en: We use these relative pronouns in relative clauses:

zh: who (有时用 that) 用来指代人:
en: who (and sometimes that) to talk about people;

zh: 我不喜欢乱扔垃圾的人。
en: I don't like people who throw litter.

zh: which (和 that) 用来指代物、地点等:
en: which (and that) to talk about things, places, etc.:

zh: 位于城镇中心的公园有美丽的大树。
en: The park which is in the centre of town has beautiful, big trees.

zh: whose 用来指代所属关系:
en: whose to talk about possession:

zh: 我遇到了那个他母亲在我们学校教书的男孩。
en: I met the boy whose mother teaches at our school.

zh: 关系代词 which, that 或 who 在定语从句中作宾语时可以省略，作主语时不可以省略:
en: We can omit the relative pronouns which, that or who in relative clauses when it is the object of the relative clause, but not when it is the subject:

zh: 我们将在我们大多数人最喜欢的地方野餐。
en: We'll have our picnic in the place (which / that) most of us like best.

zh: 你遇到的那个女孩是彼得的妹妹。
en: The girl (who) you met is Peter's sister.

zh: 我是一名高中新生。
en: I'm a new senior secondary school student.

zh: 作一名医生意味着我总是要做出正确的决定，这给我带来很大的压力。
en: Being a doctor means I have to make correct decisions all the time, which puts a lot of pressure on me.

zh: 虽然作医生使我每天非常忙碌，没有空闲时间，但是我不后悔，因为我喜欢解决问题，我能帮助人们从疾病和伤痛中康复。
en: Although being a doctor keeps me very busy, with little time for leisure, I have no regrets as I love solving problems and I get to help people recover from illnesses and injuries.

zh: 从好的方面来说，由于要参加比赛，我得去许多不同的国家。当然，听到人们为你欢呼喝彩，感觉棒极了！
en: On the plus side, I get to travel a lot to different countries for matches and of course, hearing people cheer you on is great!

zh: 静以修身，俭以养德。
en: Quietude promotes learning, frugality cultivates virtue.

zh: 非淡泊无以明志，非宁静无以致远。
en: One can't show high ideals without simple living; one can't have lofty aspirations without a peaceful state of mind.

zh: 购物前，能够比较不同的网络商店的质量和价格是非常方便的。
en: It's so convenient to be able to compare the quality and prices from different online shops before I buy.

zh: 我父母担心我会成为一个网迷。
en: My parents are worried that I may become an "Internet addict".

zh: 在他们看来，我可能无法判断这些人是否是真正的朋友，这是危险的。
en: According to them, there is a danger that I may not be able to tell whether these friends are real friends.

zh: 妈妈一直让我和学校的朋友们出去玩（而不是在网上和朋友聊天）。
en: My mum keeps telling me to go out with my school friends instead.

zh: 事实上，我非常清楚我有时需要强迫自己不上网，特别是因为现实生活也可以一样有趣。
en: Actually I do know I need to drag myself away from the online world sometimes, especially because real life can be just as interesting.

zh: 我在所有课上总是专心听讲并积极思考，这样放学后我就有更多可支配的时间做我感兴趣的事情。
en: I am always attentive in all classes and think actively, so that I can have more free time to do other things that I'm interested in after school.

zh: 但是通常来讲，这是由于工作或学习的压力。
en: But more often than not, it’s due to pressure from work or study.

zh: 经过漫长的一天，张天终于回到了他的小房间，感到疲惫。
en: After a long day, Zhang Tian finally got back to his small room, feeling tired.

zh: 来贵州教书对他来说是一次不寻常的体验。
en: Coming to Guizhou Province to teach has been quite an experience for him.

zh: 在学校初期他遇见了一些来自小村庄的好教师，他被他们所鼓舞，到最需要的地方去教学。
en: He had met wonderful teachers from small villages during his early school years and he was inspired by them to go and teach where he was needed the most.

zh: 他被作者的热情感染了。
en: He is inspired by the author's enthusiasm.

zh: 但是，不是每件事都像张天希望的那样。
en: However, not everything lived up to Zhang Tian's hopes.

zh: 离开的想法曾在脑中闪现过，但是他很快放弃了这个念头，并且找到了应对挑战的办法。
en: The thought of leaving once flashed through his mind, but he quickly gave up on the idea and found ways to deal with the challenges.

zh: 让他感到欣慰的是他的学生能够读英语、说英语和写英语，并且在学习上变得更自信。
en: What made him feel satisfied was that his students were able to read, speak and write in English, and they became more confident in learning.

zh: 一支球队的球员通过发球过网到对方场地来开始比赛。
en: A player from one team begins the game by serving the ball over the net into the other court.

zh: 人的健全，不但靠饮食，尤靠运动。
en: One's health and wellness are dependent not only on diet, but also on sport and exercise.

zh: 博格斯只有1.6米高，这使他成为NBA有史以来最矮的球员。
en: Bogues was only 1.6 metres tall, which made him the shortest player ever in the NBA.

zh: 保罗多次参加选拔，方才入选球队。
en: Paul had to try out many times just for making the team.

zh: 然而，他仍旧通常“坐板凳”作为替补队员，这对他来说是真够倒霉的。
en: He was still usually on the bench, being just a replacement, which was really tough on him.

zh: “别让我们失望，保罗。你发光发亮的时刻到了。”
en: "Don't let us down, Paul. It's your time to shine."

zh: “，”教练拍着保罗的肩膀说，“你刚刚赢得了在球队中的一席之地，大个子！”
en: "Well," said the coach as he hit Paul on the shoulder, "you've just earned your place on the team, big guy!"

zh: 粉丝们在通往终点的路边排开，为选手们加油。
en: Fans lined the road to the finishing line, cheering on the competitors.

zh: 两位自行车手展现出了高度的体育精神—纳瓦罗拒绝利用埃斯特班碰到的麻烦……
en: The two cyclists made amazing sporting gestures — Navarro for refusing to take advantage of Esteban's troubles.

zh: 你不该利用他的善良，予取予求。
en: You should not take advantage of his kindness by demanding too much.

zh: 爆竹声中一岁除，春风送暖入屠苏。
en: Amid the boom of firecrackers a year has come to an end, and the spring wind has wafted warm breath to the wine.

zh: 首先，房子从上到下都打扫了一遍。
en: First, the house was cleaned from top to bottom.

zh: 我的房东妈妈陈太太说这是清除过去一年的积尘，为新的一年做准备。
en: My host mother Mrs Chen said this was to sweep away the dirt of the past year and get ready for the new year.

zh: 每年，一坐上火车我就会被山西方言包围，我意识到我踏上了回家的路。
en: Every year, the moment I get on the train I am surrounded by Shanxi accents — I know that I am heading home, to my family.

zh: 我不能经常回山西，因此每年回到家，我父母会把这一年发生的事一股脑儿地告诉我，谁结婚了，谁有小孩了，谁上大学了，等等。
en: I don't get to travel back to Shanxi very often, so when I get home, my parents will fill me in on what's been happening — who has got married or had children or gone away to university.

zh: 努力和其他人交谈也很重要。
en: It's also important to make an effort to talk to other people.

zh: 我们不想占用你太多时间。
en: Well, we don't want to take up too much of your time.

zh: 我记得那天的风，因为奶奶到达时她的灰头发乱作一团。
en: I remember the wind because Granny's grey hair was a mess when she arrived.

zh: 在圣诞前夜，奶奶在火炉旁坐下，我们则搭起了圣诞树。
en: On Christmas Eve, Granny took a seat by the fire as we put up the Christmas tree.

zh: 我们戴上傻气的纸帽子，吃塞有土豆的大火鸡，随后又吃圣诞布丁。
en: We put on silly paper hats and had a big turkey with potatoes, followed by Christmas pudding.

zh: 大卫在嘴里塞了太多布丁，以至于都咽不下去了。
en: David put so much pudding in his mouth that he couldn't swallow.

zh: 我是一名新的高中学生。
en: I'm a new senior secondary school student.

zh: 这是我人生中新篇章的开始。
en: It is the start of a new chapter in my life.

zh: 我的新学校生活非常令人兴奋。
en: My new school life is very exciting.

zh: 我感到兴奋是因为这所学校在很多方面与我以前的学校不同。
en: I feel excited because this school differs in many ways from my previous one.

zh: 以前，我们有固定的教室，学生们都成排坐着。
en: Previously, we had fixed classrooms where students all sat in rows.

zh: 在我的新学校，我们没有固定的教室。
en: In my new school, we don't have fixed classrooms.

zh: 学生们围坐成小组，我们经常进行小组讨论，分享我们的想法或一起检查我们的作业。
en: Students sit around in groups and we often have group discussions and share our ideas or check our work together.

zh: 另一个很大的不同是，同一个班的学生可以有不同课程表。
en: What is also very different is that students in the same class can have different class schedules.

zh: 这意味着我们为自己选择的不同科目去不同的教室。
en: That means we go to different classrooms for different subjects of our own choices.

zh: 在接下来的三年里，我希望我能学到许多新东西。
en: In the next three years, I hope I will learn many new things.

zh: 我也希望我能培养说英语的信心，并发展多种新技能的能力。
en: I also hope I will develop confidence in speaking English and develop competence in a variety of new skills.

zh: 总而言之，我希望在我的新学校拥有一个更快乐、更充实、更令人满意的生活。
en: All in all, I hope to have a happier, more fruitful and satisfying life in my new school.

zh: 嗯，医生的生活可能压力很大，因为我有很多病人要看。
en: Well, a doctor's life can be quite stressful as I have many patients to see.

zh: 我通常早上6:30离开公寓，早上七点前到达工作地点。
en: I usually leave my apartment at 6.30 am and get to work by seven in the morning.

zh: 我在医院轮班工作12小时。
en: I work 12-hour shifts in the hospital.

zh: 作为一名医生意味着我必须一直做出正确的决定，这给我带来了很大压力。
en: Being a doctor means I have to make correct decisions all the time, which puts a lot of pressure on me.

zh: 虽然当医生让我非常忙碌，几乎没有闲暇时间，但我并不后悔，因为我喜欢解决问题，并且能够帮助人们从疾病和伤痛中康复。
en: Although being a doctor keeps me very busy, with little time for leisure, I have no regrets as I love solving problems and I get to help people recover from illnesses and injuries.

zh: 出名并不像你想的那么容易。
en: Being famous isn't as easy as you think.

zh: 作为一名足球运动员，我一年必须训练10个月，每天五个小时。
en: As a football player I have to train for 10 months a year, five hours a day.

zh: 我可能在训练或比赛中受伤，那是最糟糕的！
en: I can get injured while training or playing and that's the worst!

zh: 从好的方面看，我可以经常去不同的国家参加比赛，当然，听到人们为你加油是很棒的！
en: On the plus side, I get to travel a lot to different countries for matches and of course, hearing people cheer you on is great!

zh: 作为一名业务经理，我大多数日子都是从早上8点到深夜都在办公室。
en: Being a business manager, I'm in the office most days from 8 am until late in the evening.

zh: 作为一名经理意味着我可以做出重要的决定，我真的很享受这一点。
en: Being a manager means I get to make important decisions and I really enjoy that.

zh: 不幸的是，这份工作也可能有压力，尤其是当挑战出现时，我工作太多，以至于觉得没有足够的时间陪伴家人。
en: Unfortunately, the job can also be stressful, especially when challenges arise and I work so much that I feel I don't see my family enough.

zh: 这是我工作中最糟糕的部分。
en: That's the worst part of my job.

zh: 今天我们将探讨压力这个话题——什么是
en: Today we're going to look at the topic of stress — what

zh: 导致它的原因以及我们可以做些什么来放松。
en: causes it and what we can do to relax.

zh: 我们请来了压力专家金博士。
en: We have Dr. King with us, an expert on stress.

zh: 那么，什么样的人经常承受压力？
en: So, what kind of people often suffer from stress?

zh: 嗯，坦白说，每个人在人生的某个阶段都会承受压力——教师、医生、工厂工人、报纸编辑……
en: Well, to be frank, everyone suffers from stress at some time in their life — teachers, doctors, factory workers, newspaper editors ...

zh: 我们无法将压力从我们的生活中完全移除。
en: We can’t remove stress from our lives entirely.

zh: 然而，有些工作可能比其他工作压力更大，比如警察或军队中的某些职位。
en: However, some jobs may be more stressful than others, like some positions in the police or the military.

zh: 但究竟是什么导致了压力？
en: But what really causes stress?

zh: 但通常，这是由于工作或学习的压力造成的。
en: But more often than not, it's due to pressure from work or study.

zh: 那么，举个例子，学生呢？
en: So, for example, what about students?

zh: 有很多作业的学生会感到压力很大。
en: Students with lots of homework can feel very stressed.

zh: 他们可能会觉得没有足够的时间完成所有事情。
en: They may feel they don't have enough time to finish everything.

zh: 我相信我们作为学生都经历过那种压力。
en: I'm sure that we have all experienced that kind of stress as students.

zh: 你认为社会压力在多大程度上会导致压力？
en: To what extent do you think that social pressure can cause stress?

zh: 嗯，害羞的人可能会觉得社交场合压力很大——例如，去参加聚会。
en: Well, shy people can find social situations very stressful — going to parties, for example.

zh: 因此，回答你的问题，社交场合对某些人来说可能压力非常大。
en: Thus, to answer your question, social situations can be very stressful for some people.

zh: 我们能做些什么来停止压力和紧张？
en: And what can we do to stop stress and tension?

zh: 有很多方法可以减轻压力。
en: There are many ways to reduce stress.

zh: 你可以组织你的工作或学习。
en: You can organise your work or studies.

zh: 换句话说，你可以列一个清单，先做所有重要的事情。
en: In other words, you can make a list and do all the important things first.

zh: 你必须吃好并经常锻炼。
en: You must eat well and exercise often.

zh: 此外，你可以和人谈谈你的问题——和朋友、老师或家人谈谈。
